{"parentUuid":"f87e1130-179c-42f0-a6f6-cb9f4923561d","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_015kd2oxo6rqt3YH5jZE2y4f","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Polities Research Report: Methodology and Sources\n     2→\n     3→**Date**: 2026-03-24\n     4→**Branch**: lbm364dl/refactor-polities\n     5→**Scope**: Polities from 1860 to present day\n     6→**Author**: Automated analysis cross-referencing multiple datasets\n     7→\n     8→## 1. Objective\n     9→\n    10→Systematically verify the WHEP polities database (polity_codes.csv, 603 entries) against\n    11→historical records, identifying:\n    12→- Incorrect start/end years\n    13→- Missing polities (entities with distinct territories not represented)\n    14→- Unnecessary duplicates\n    15→- Timeline gaps and overlaps\n    16→- Potential territorial changes not captured\n    17→\n    18→## 2. Data Sources Consulted\n    19→\n    20→### 2.1 Internal Sources (this branch)\n    21→| Source | File | Entries | Coverage |\n    22→|--------|------|---------|----------|\n    23→| Federico-Tena | federico_tena_polities.csv | 244 | 1800-1938 |\n    24→| FAOSTAT | faostat_regions.csv | 344 | 1961-present |\n    25→| M49 (UN) | historical_m49.csv | 286 | 1970-present |\n    26→| CShapes 2.0 | cshapes.csv | 316 | 1886-2019 |\n    27→| WHEP fixes | whep_fixes.csv | 17 | Manual overrides |\n    28→| Polity codes | polity_codes.csv | 603 | Combined output |\n    29→| Common names | common_names.csv | ~1200 | Source mappings |\n    30→\n    31→### 2.2 Colleague's Database ('/home/catalin/Downloads/polities data full.xlsx')\n    32→| Sheet | Entries | Content |\n    33→|-------|---------|---------|\n    34→| whep_equivalences | 1226 rows, 179 unique polities | FT + CShapes + COW merged |\n    35→| COWs full entities changes | 381 | Territorial transfers with areas |\n    36→| COWs edited to merge with CShapes | ~1000 | Adapted COW for CShapes merge |\n    37→| Europe from 1850 | ~240 polity-periods | Detailed European state periods with areas |\n    38→| Cliopatria | 2905 (2694 from 1860+) | Historical polities with areas |\n    39→| Colonies_ft_cshapes | ~1000 | Colonial periods mapped to modern states |\n    40→| COW-country-codes | 244 | Correlates of War country codes |\n    41→| COWs Entities info | 2707 | Full COW entity list |\n    42→| British/French/Ottoman empire sheets | Various | Empire-specific entity histories |\n    43→\n    44→### 2.3 External References\n    45→- **Correlates of War (COW) Project**: Territorial Change dataset v6\n    46→- **CShapes 2.0**: ETH Zurich territorial mapping (1886-2019)\n    47→- **Cliopatria**: Historical polities database (via colleague's Excel)\n    48→- **UN M49**: United Nations Standard Country Codes\n    49→- **FAOSTAT**: FAO country definitions\n    50→- **CIA World Factbook** (area references in colleague's notes)\n    51→\n    52→## 3. Methodology\n    53→\n    54→### 3.1 Definition of \"Polity\"\n    55→Per WHEP project: A polity is a **fixed territory over a continuous period of time**.\n    56→- Territory change = new polity (even if same country name)\n    57→- Name change only (no territory change) = same polity\n    58→- Most colonies tracked separately if they have individual trade data\n    59→\n    60→### 3.2 Polity Code Format\n    61→`XXX-yyyy-YYYY` where:\n    62→- `XXX` = ISO3 code or artificial code\n    63→- `yyyy` = start year\n    64→- `YYYY` = end year\n    65→\n    66→### 3.3 Verification Process\n    67→1. Parse all 603 polity codes and extract year ranges\n    68→2. Group by ISO prefix to detect overlaps/gaps\n    69→3. Cross-reference each era (1860-1920, 1920-1945, 1945-1975, 1975-1991, 1991-2025)\n    70→   against COW territorial changes, CShapes boundaries, and Cliopatria records\n    71→4. Compare with colleague's whep_equivalences working sheet\n    72→5. Flag discrepancies with severity levels:\n    73→   - **CRITICAL**: Wrong dates affecting data joins\n    74→   - **IMPORTANT**: Missing polity periods for significant territorial changes\n    75→   - **MINOR**: Design choices that could be revisited\n    76→   - **NOTE**: Observations for documentation\n    77→\n    78→### 3.4 Area Threshold\n    79→Per colleague's delimitations sheet:\n    80→- Territorial change >= 10% of total polity area = new polity boundary\n    81→- Islands: change >= 100 km2 = new boundary\n    82→- Islands assumed same area through history if single polity\n    83→"}]},"uuid":"d44e22c8-27dd-43fa-ab06-1a1475a58a4a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:06:37.003Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"f87e1130-179c-42f0-a6f6-cb9f4923561d","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"2268bc3b-3256-49fa-b6d1-b46081ed793c","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012pA12AULuLAk4h6R4cov6f","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Iteration 2: Targeted Research Agent Findings\n     2→\n     3→## Agent 1: Denmark 1864\n     4→\n     5→**Key findings (confirmed by web research)**:\n     6→- Treaty of Vienna signed **October 30, 1864**\n     7→- Denmark lost Schleswig (~9,400 km2), Holstein (~8,417 km2), Lauenburg (~1,183 km2) = **~19,000 km2 total**\n     8→- Denmark before: **~58,000 km2** (European territory), after: **~39,000 km2**\n     9→- Loss: **~33% of territory, ~40% of population**\n    10→- North Schleswig returned 1920 via plebiscite: **+3,984 km2**\n    11→- CShapes starts at 1886, so the 1864 change is already past by then\n    12→\n    13→**Conclusion**: Confirmed as CShapes limitation. The 1864 change was massive (33%) and\n    14→would warrant a new polity period per WHEP rules, but requires pre-1886 geometry data\n    15→that CShapes doesn't have. The colleague's \"Europe from 1850\" sheet has the data\n    16→(Denmark 1816-1864: 57,086 km2; Denmark 1865-1885: 38,483 km2).\n    17→\n    18→**Additional finding**: Denmark also lost Norway to Sweden in 1814 (Treaty of Kiel),\n    19→sold the Danish West Indies to the US in 1917, and Iceland became sovereign in 1918\n    20→(full independence 1944). None of these additional changes affect the current database.\n    21→\n    22→## Agent 2: Two Sicilies Duplicate\n    23→\n    24→**Key findings (confirmed by web research)**:\n    25→- \"Kingdom of the Two Sicilies\" and \"Two Sicilies\" are the **full name and short name\n    26→  for the EXACT same entity**\n    27→- COW codes it as \"Two Sicilies\" (SIC, #329) — **single entry**\n    28→- CShapes does NOT include it (ended 1860, before CShapes 1886 start)\n    29→- The FT source paper (Federico & Tena-Junguito 2019) draws its polity list from COW\n    30→- The duplicate arose from two separate entries in the FT CSV for the same entity\n    31→\n    32→**Conclusion**: Our fix is confirmed correct. Both FT entries now map to common name\n    33→\"Two Sicilies\" and the duplicate polity_code KIN-1800-1860 has been removed.\n    34→\n    35→## Agent 3: Ionian Islands Date\n    36→\n    37→**Key findings (confirmed by web research)**:\n    38→- Treaty of London signed **November 14, 1863** (not 1864 as some sources say)\n    39→- Actual handover: **May 28, 1864**\n    40→- CShapes does NOT include the Ionian Islands at all (transfer predates CShapes 1886 coverage)\n    41→- The 1862 date comes **exclusively from Federico-Tena**\n    42→- What happened in 1862: **King Otto of Greece was deposed** in October 1862, triggering\n    43→  negotiations that led to Britain offering the Ionians to the new Greek government\n    44→- The FT data itself is contradictory: end_year=1862 but notes=\"1864-1938 Greece\"\n    45→\n    46→**Nuance**: The agent noted that FT may be using 1862 as \"last year of separate trade\n    47→data availability\" rather than the political transfer date. This is plausible — trade\n    48→statistics may have been disrupted after the 1862 Greek revolution.\n    49→\n    50→**Decision**: Our fix (end_year=1864) is historically accurate for the political\n    51→transfer. If FT specifically tracks trade data cessation, 1862 might be defensible,\n    52→but the FT notes saying \"1864-1938 Greece\" suggest they intended 1864 as the transition.\n    53→**Keep the fix.**\n    54→\n    55→## Agent 4: Additional Databases\n    56→\n    57→Found **24 additional datasets** beyond the ones already known. Most relevant:\n    58→\n    59→| Dataset | Geometry? | Coverage | Free? |\n    60→|---------|-----------|----------|-------|\n    61→| **Aourednik Historical Basemaps** | Yes (GeoJSON) | 1880-1960 snapshots | Yes |\n    62→| **MPIDR Census Mosaic** (Max Planck) | Yes (Shapefiles) | 1860-2003, Europe | Yes |\n    63→| **COW Territorial Change v6** | No (tabular) | 1816-2018 | Yes |\n    64→| **Wimmer & Min Empire-to-Nation** | No (tabular) | 1816-2001 | Yes |\n    65→| **V-Dem Historical** | No (tabular) | 1789-present | Yes |\n    66→| **Thenmap API** | Yes (GeoJSON) | 1945-present | Yes |\n    67→| **IBAD (Border Agreements)** | No (tabular) | 1816-2001 | Yes |\n    68→| **Euratlas** | Yes (Shapefiles) | 1CE-2000CE | Paid |\n    69→| **IPE Data Resource** | No (tabular) | 1800-2018 | Yes |\n    70→\n    71→**Recommendation for the Denmark 1864 gap**: The **Aourednik Historical Basemaps**\n    72→have an 1880 snapshot with GeoJSON polygons that would include pre-1920 Denmark\n    73→(already without Schleswig-Holstein but correct for that period). The **MPIDR Census\n    74→Mosaic** may have the actual 1860-era Danish boundaries. These could potentially\n    75→provide the missing geometry.\n    76→\n    77→Full database survey saved in `13_additional_databases.md`.\n    78→\n    79→## Agent 5: Orange Free State\n    80→\n    81→**Confirmed our analysis**: Two entries from different sources (FT: 1848-1900, CShapes:\n    82→pre-1886 to 1910) represent different perspectives. The independent republic ended at\n    83→the Boer War (1900/1902), but the territory continued as British Orange River Colony\n    84→until the Union of South Africa (1910). CShapes tracks the territory, FT tracks trade.\n    85→\n    86→**No action needed.** The overlap is by design.\n    87→\n    88→## Agent 6: Sweden\n    89→\n    90→**Key findings**:\n    91→- Norway separated in **1905** (dissolution of union June 7, 1905)\n    92→- Sweden before: **761,932 km2** (including Norway), after: **443,303 km2**\n    93→- Loss: **41.8%** of territory\n    94→- CShapes correctly has two periods at exactly 1905\n    95→- FAOSTAT, M49, and FT all treat Sweden as a single entity with no temporal split\n    96→\n    97→**Important nuance**: The agent identified a **semantic inconsistency**:\n    98→- SWE-1800-2025 claims to cover 1800-2025 but its geometry is post-1905 only (443K km2)\n    99→- SWE-1800-1905 correctly has the larger pre-1905 geometry (762K km2)\n   100→- For the period 1800-1905, SWE-1800-2025's geometry doesn't match Sweden's actual territory\n   101→\n   102→The agent suggests this could be resolved by adding a whep_fix to set Sweden's start\n   103→to 1905 (like was done for Russia 2014 and Serbia 2008), but this would break the\n   104→FAOSTAT/M49/FT linkage which uses \"Sweden\" as a continuous entity back to 1800.\n   105→\n   106→**Decision**: Keep current design. The SWE-1800-2025 entity serves FAOSTAT/M49\n   107→data linkage (trade data attributed to \"Sweden\" regardless of territorial extent).\n   108→SWE-1800-1905 provides the correct larger geometry for spatial analysis. Downstream\n   109→users should use the appropriate polity depending on whether they need trade data\n   110→linkage or territorial accuracy. **Document this clearly.**\n   111→"}]},"uuid":"9840fd05-5d2c-4f3b-9f47-b4cfaa52e52e","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:06:37.994Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"2268bc3b-3256-49fa-b6d1-b46081ed793c","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"b4cae259-a781-4311-80fc-3a79fe93547b","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_011JC1fQ8iJcZ6JEnRLnLusT","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Polities Research: Iteration 2 Findings\n     2→\n     3→**Date**: 2026-03-24 (second pass)\n     4→\n     5→## Summary of Changes from First Pass\n     6→\n     7→The second-pass deep-dive confirmed the first-pass findings and uncovered additional\n     8→issues through detailed source tracing and systematic scanning.\n     9→\n    10→## New Findings\n    11→\n    12→### CONFIRMED BUG: Two Sicilies Duplicate (UPGRADED from \"investigate\" to \"confirmed\")\n    13→\n    14→**Root cause**: The Federico-Tena source dataset contains TWO rows for the same entity:\n    15→- Row 1: \"Kingdom Two sicilies\" (start=NA, end=1860, notes=\"1861-1938 Italy; joint estimation with Italy\")\n    16→- Row 2: \"Two Sicilies\" (start=NA, end=1860, notes=\"1861-1938 Italy; joint estimation with Italy\")\n    17→\n    18→Both rows have IDENTICAL dates and notes. Neither appears in any other source (CShapes,\n    19→FAOSTAT, M49). The common_names.csv maps each to a different common name, producing\n    20→two separate polity codes:\n    21→- KIN-1800-1860 (\"Kingdom Two sicilies\")\n    22→- TWO-1800-1860 (\"Two Sicilies\")\n    23→\n    24→**This is a data duplication bug in the FT source that propagated through the pipeline.**\n    25→\n    26→**Fix required**: Remove one entry from common_names.csv and polity_codes.csv.\n    27→Recommend keeping TWO-1800-1860 and removing KIN-1800-1860.\n    28→\n    29→### NEW BUG: Ionian Islands End Year (UPGRADED from \"minor\" to \"confirmed error\")\n    30→\n    31→**Root cause**: Federico-Tena has end_year=1862 for Ionian Islands, but the FT notes\n    32→themselves say \"1864-1938 Greece.\" This is an internal contradiction in the FT data.\n    33→\n    34→**Historical facts**:\n    35→- Ionian Parliament votes for union with Greece: October 7, 1863\n    36→- Treaty of London signed: November 14, 1863\n    37→- Handover to Greece: May 28, 1864\n    38→\n    39→The year 1862 doesn't correspond to any known event. It is likely a transcription error\n    40→in the original FT dataset. The correct end year should be **1864** (year of actual handover).\n    41→\n    42→**Fix required**: Add to whep_fixes.csv with end_year=1864. Update polity_codes.csv\n    43→from ION-1815-1862 to ION-1815-1864.\n    44→\n    45→### NEW BUG: Kokand End Year (NEW FINDING)\n    46→\n    47→**Root cause**: Federico-Tena has end_year=1883 for Kokand, but the Khanate of Kokand\n    48→was conquered by Russia in **1876**. The FT notes say \"1873-1938 Russia and Soviet Union\"\n    49→which adds to the confusion.\n    50→\n    51→**Historical facts**:\n    52→- Russian military campaigns begin: 1873\n    53→- Khanate of Kokand formally dissolved by Russian Empire: February 19, 1876\n    54→- Incorporated into Russian Turkestan as Fergana Oblast\n    55→\n    56→The year 1883 is 7 years after the actual dissolution. The notes saying \"1873\" refer to\n    57→the start of Russian military campaigns, not the actual end of Kokand. COW data records\n    58→Kokand conquered in 1873 at 92,463 km².\n    59→\n    60→**Fix required**: Add to whep_fixes.csv with end_year=1876. Update polity_codes.csv\n    61→from KOK-1800-1883 to KOK-1800-1876.\n    62→\n    63→### CLARIFIED: Orange Free State (Downgraded from \"issue\" to \"design ambiguity\")\n    64→\n    65→**Root cause**: Two separate sources, correctly producing two separate polities:\n    66→- ORA-1848-1900: FT trade data for the independent republic (ends at Boer War)\n    67→- ORA-1800-1910: CShapes territorial entity (includes British colony period 1902-1910)\n    68→\n    69→These overlap from 1848-1900 but have different common names and represent genuinely\n    70→different perspectives (trade entity vs. territorial entity).\n    71→\n    72→**Decision**: No fix needed. This is the same pattern as other \"aggregate + detailed\"\n    73→entries (like Ethiopia (old) + Ethiopia periods). Document as design choice.\n    74→\n    75→### CLARIFIED: Sweden (Confirmed correct)\n    76→\n    77→**Root cause**: CShapes produces two periods (pre/post 1905 Norway independence).\n    78→The FAOSTAT/M49/FT sources produce a single \"Sweden\" entity spanning 1800-2025.\n    79→The pipeline correctly aggregates them into:\n    80→- SWE-1800-1905: CShapes pre-Norway-separation territory (761,932 km²)\n    81→- SWE-1800-2025: Modern Sweden aggregate (M49/FAOSTAT codes + post-1905 geometry, 443,303 km²)\n    82→\n    83→**Decision**: Correct by design. No fix needed.\n    84→\n    85→### CLARIFIED: Denmark 1864 (Confirmed as CShapes limitation)\n    86→\n    87→**Root cause**: CShapes 2.0 starts at 1886. Denmark lost Schleswig-Holstein in 1864\n    88→(33% territory loss: 57,086 km² → 38,483 km²). By 1886, this change had already occurred.\n    89→\n    90→The colleague's \"Europe from 1850\" sheet has the pre-1864 Denmark data (57,086 km²)\n    91→from a different source, but this data is not integrated into the WHEP pipeline.\n    92→\n    93→**Decision**: Cannot fix without adding a new data source for pre-1886 geometry.\n    94→Document as known limitation of CShapes 2.0 coverage.\n    95→\n    96→## Additional Scan Results\n    97→\n    98→### FT Date Warnings (non-issues)\n    99→The systematic scan found many FT entries where the notes mention a successor year\n   100→that is exactly end_year + 1. These are all CORRECT:\n   101→- Duchy Modena: end=1860, notes \"1861-1938 Italy\" (1860+1=1861, correct)\n   102→- Duchy Parma: end=1860, notes \"1861-1938 Italy\" (correct)\n   103→- Sardinia: end=1860, notes \"1861-1938 Italy\" (correct)\n   104→- Papal States: end=1870, notes \"1871-1938 Italy\" (correct)\n   105→- Tuscany: end=1860, notes \"1861-1938 Italy\" (correct)\n   106→- Crete: end=1913, notes \"1914-1938 Greece\" (correct)\n   107→\n   108→Only Ionian Islands (end=1862, notes \"1864-1938\") and Kokand (end=1883, notes \"1873-1938\")\n   109→have genuine mismatches.\n   110→\n   111→### New Guinea Entries (non-issue)\n   112→The scan found similar-named FT entries:\n   113→- \"British New Guinea\" (1884-NA): Present-day Papua New Guinea\n   114→- \"New Guinea\" (1884-NA): German colony\n   115→- \"Dutch new Guinea\" (1898-NA): Present-day Indonesian Papua\n   116→\n   117→These are CORRECTLY three different entities controlled by different colonial powers.\n   118→\n   119→### CShapes Mapping Completeness\n   120→- All 187 unique CShapes country names have rename_cshapes.csv mappings\n   121→- All 315 CShapes polity-period entries have common_names.csv mappings\n   122→- **100% CShapes coverage confirmed**\n   123→\n   124→### Area Comparison\n   125→CShapes areas match the colleague's \"Europe from 1850\" data within <1% for all\n   126→overlapping periods (post-1886). This confirms data integrity.\n   127→\n   128→## Updated Issue Tracker\n   129→\n   130→| # | Issue | Severity | Status | Action |\n   131→|---|-------|----------|--------|--------|\n   132→| 1 | Manchukuo end year 2025→1945 | IMPORTANT | **FIXED** (pass 1) | whep_fixes.csv updated |\n   133→| 2 | Two Sicilies duplicate (FT) | IMPORTANT | **CONFIRMED BUG** | Need to remove duplicate |\n   134→| 3 | Ionian Islands end 1862→1864 | IMPORTANT | **CONFIRMED BUG** | Need whep_fixes + code update |\n   135→| 4 | Kokand end 1883→1876 | IMPORTANT | **NEW BUG** | Need whep_fixes + code update |\n   136→| 5 | Denmark 1864 missing | MINOR | CShapes limitation | Document only |\n   137→| 6 | Orange Free State duplicate | IMPORTANT | **CONFIRMED BUG** | Merged (like Transvaal) |\n   138→| 7 | Sweden two entries | NOT A BUG | Correct by design | Document only |\n   139→"}]},"uuid":"8310a334-8a65-4b98-8518-9b3846541d57","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:06:38.209Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"b4cae259-a781-4311-80fc-3a79fe93547b","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"b1f64db4-313a-46f7-8a23-2d2dcd4aa26b","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01E159U4xfMFW76cNEf5BbcT","type":"tool_result","content":"# GADM Information Based on Available Content\n\nBased on the webpage provided, I can only offer limited answers:\n\n**What I can confirm:**\n- GADM \"provides maps and spatial data for all countries and their sub-divisions\"\n- Users can browse maps or download data to create their own maps\n- The site has dedicated sections for [Maps](/maps.html) and [Data](/data.html)\n\n**What I cannot answer from this content:**\nThe webpage excerpt does not address:\n- Whether historical boundaries are included or only current administrative divisions\n- Available data formats\n- Pricing or free/paid status\n- Specific spatial coverage details\n- Whether modern boundaries can proxy historical territories\n\nTo get answers about historical data availability, file formats, licensing, coverage details, and methodological questions, you would need to visit the full GADM website, particularly the \"About\" section and Data/Maps pages referenced in this excerpt."}]},"uuid":"f56e7a5e-1318-492c-9b7b-d9d885c00791","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:06:56.450Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"b1f64db4-313a-46f7-8a23-2d2dcd4aa26b","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"09987195-15be-4fe8-8e59-a38d8150637a","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01D2jBrffy5qkSzkMcBhn2n9","type":"tool_result","content":"# Natural Earth Political/Admin Boundary Polygon Layers\n\n## Available Polygon Layers\n\nNatural Earth offers several political and administrative boundary polygon datasets:\n\n1. **Admin 0 – Countries**: \"There are 258 countries in the world\" including Greenland as separate from Denmark.\n\n2. **Admin 0 – Details**: Includes \"298 cartographic map units and 360 subunits optimized for labeling edge cases\" such as the constituent countries of the UK.\n\n3. **Admin 0 – Breakaway, Disputed Areas**: Covers approximately 100 disputed territories, described as covering features \"from Kashmir to the Elemi Triangle.\"\n\n4. **Admin 1 – States, Provinces**: Internal administrative divisions representing \"states, provinces\" within countries.\n\n## Coverage Scope\n\nThe dataset includes dependent territories (Greenland), disputed areas, and small islands within the 258 countries representation. However, the page doesn't explicitly mention historical territories or colonies.\n\n## Data Quality & Format\n\nLayers feature \"carefully generalized linework\" maintaining geographic accuracy at 1:10m, 1:50m, and 1:110m scales. Data includes embedded feature names ranked by importance.\n\n## Availability & Licensing\n\nThe data is **completely free**: \"Natural Earth is a public domain map dataset\" available for \"use in any type of project.\"\n\nSpecific file formats aren't detailed on this homepage, though the site offers downloads across multiple scales."}]},"uuid":"af7ec517-08c6-49a4-bdf9-5e5c9ec5c8fb","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:07:01.599Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"09987195-15be-4fe8-8e59-a38d8150637a","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Weidmann, Lars-Erik Cederman, Kristian Skrede Gleditsch, 2022\",\"url\":\"https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/full/10.1177/00220027211013563\"},{\"title\":\"CShapes Dataset of Historical Country Boundaries\",\"url\":\"https://demo.ldproxy.net/cshapes\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - Research Repository\",\"url\":\"https://repository.essex.ac.uk/31791/\"},{\"title\":\"The CShapes 2.0 Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://d-nb.info/125016981X/34\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://ideas.repec.org/a/sae/jocore/v66y2022i1p144-161.html\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - R Discovery\",\"url\":\"https://discovery.researcher.life/article/mapping-the-international-system-1886-2019-the-cshapes-2-0-dataset/fe9ded6b9fff361783efa982604cc051\"},{\"title\":\"(PDF) Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/351339723_Mapping_the_International_System_1886-2019_The_CShapes_20_Dataset\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about the CShapes 2.0 dataset regarding dependent territories, colonies, and sovereign states:\n\n## Dataset Coverage\n\nCShapes 2.0 maps the borders and capitals of independent states and dependent territories from 1886 to 2019. A key improvement from the previous version is that the new dataset is no longer limited to independent states, but also maps the borders of colonies and other dependencies, thereby providing near complete global coverage of political units throughout recent history.\n\n## Unit Definitions\n\nThe dataset considers two types of units: independent states and dependent territories. For the former category, most political science research has relied on two main datasets of independent states, each with its own definition of statehood: the Correlates of War (COW) list and the Gleditsch and Ward (1999) list of independent states (GW).\n\nThere are two versions of the dataset, which are based on the Gleditsch and Ward (1999) or the Correlates of War coding of independent states.\n\n## Historical Coverage\n\nTo code changes due to the creation and dissolution of units, the coding relies on the GW and COW lists of states and the COW list of dependencies, which are used to track the historical lifespans of political units. For transfers of sovereignty over units as a whole, the dataset also relies on the COW list of dependencies, which keeps track of changes in sovereignty over dependent territories.\n\nThe documentation for CShapes 2.0 is available through the official ETH Zurich International Conflict Research group website and has been published in the Journal of Conflict Resolution.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"601e2ca8-e2bd-4d0c-9e42-789a5766e88f","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:04.453Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"66bc89b1-7c21-4b59-a41c-6ddb600235cc","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"2fe9d8e6-4df7-43b2-b444-5043efbf09ce","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KYe1fjUfxqS3YFmgDtxh3Y","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"GADM historical boundaries administrative data formats shapefile download\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"GADM\",\"url\":\"https://www.gadm.org/\"},{\"title\":\"GADM data\",\"url\":\"https://gadm.org/data.html\"},{\"title\":\"Download GADM data (version 4.1)\",\"url\":\"https://gadm.org/download_country.html\"},{\"title\":\"Download GADM data (version 3.6)\",\"url\":\"https://gadm.org/download_country_v3.html\"},{\"title\":\"Global Administrative Areas\",\"url\":\"https://atlas.co/data-sources/global-administrative-areas/\"},{\"title\":\"Download GADM data\",\"url\":\"https://gadm.org/download_world.html\"},{\"title\":\"Global Administrative Areas (GADM) | McMaster University Libraries\",\"url\":\"https://library.mcmaster.ca/maps/geospatial/global-administrative-areas-gadm\"},{\"title\":\"Global Administrative Areas (GADM) | Geospatial Centre | University of Waterloo\",\"url\":\"https://uwaterloo.ca/library/geospatial/collections/us-and-world-geospatial-data-resources/global-administrative-areas-gadm\"},{\"title\":\"Download Shapefiles For Any Country - Mapscaping.com\",\"url\":\"https://mapscaping.com/download-shapefiles-for-any-country/\"},{\"title\":\"GADM 3.6 - Administrative boundaries (level 3)\",\"url\":\"https://data.apps.fao.org/map/catalog/static/search?format=shapefile\"}]\n\n## Search Results for GADM Historical Boundaries\n\nI found comprehensive information about GADM (Global Administrative Areas) and its data offerings:\n\n### Overview\nGADM provides maps and spatial data for all countries and their sub-divisions. The current version is 4.1, which delimits 400,276 administrative areas.\n\n### Data Formats\nGADM offers data in GeoPackage, Shapefile, KMZ (Google Earth), and R spatial data objects. Additionally, data is available as a single database in the GeoPackage format (the standard format) or as a geodatabase.\n\n### Download Options\nDownloading by country is the recommended approach, though you can also download data for the entire world. Data is available for individual countries or as a complete global dataset.\n\n### Historical Versions\nOlder versions can be downloaded. Version 5 will be released in January 2026.\n\n### Administrative Levels\nThe dataset series comprises six administrative levels: National (level 0), State/province/equivalent (level 1), County/district/equivalent (level 2), Commune/municipality/equivalent (level 3) and smaller Levels 4 and 5.\n\n### Licensing\nThe data are freely available for academic use and other non-commercial use, though redistribution or commercial use is not allowed without prior permission.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"a9a06ce2-9df3-49e0-8407-ad8528a1d9f4","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:04.702Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"2fe9d8e6-4df7-43b2-b444-5043efbf09ce","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"5d286ac3-8514-46d1-bfc3-716c42ca4934","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018aPcvBgsGcUPDfU9MbkdBz","type":"tool_result","content":"# Historical Basemaps Dataset Overview\n\n## Available Years\nThe repository contains multiple year snapshots of world boundaries. Examples mentioned include 1492, 1880, and 1938, but the README doesn't provide a complete list. You can view all available years by checking the `index.json` file or browsing the geojson folder.\n\n## Format\nData is stored in **GeoJSON format** primarily. The README states: \"All maps are stored in the geojson format: single file per feature layer, human and machine readable.\" SVG versions also exist but are considered secondary derivatives for non-GIS users.\n\n## Included Polities\nThe dataset covers \"world countries and cultural regions\" across different historical periods, including empires, kingdoms, and tribal areas. Historical entities like the Inca Empire are documented.\n\n## Colonies & Dependencies\nThe dataset includes colonial territories. A key property is **SUBJECTO**, which captures \"the name of the colonial power exercising authority on the country or region.\"\n\n## Accuracy & Limitations\nThe project explicitly states it is \"work in progress\" and advises users to \"verify the maps by comparison to other sources before using in academic work.\" The README emphasizes that \"historical boundaries are even more disputed than contemporary ones\" and notes that border precision varies (rated 1-3 on the BORDERPRECISION field).\n\n## Cost\n**Yes, it's free.** The repository uses a GPL-3.0 license, making it open source and freely available."}]},"uuid":"9b4164d2-f28c-4373-ae0c-5beaa27b82b3","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:05.759Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"5d286ac3-8514-46d1-bfc3-716c42ca4934","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Free vector and raster map data at 1:10m, 1:50m, and 1:110m scales\",\"url\":\"https://www.naturalearthdata.com/downloads/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's information about Natural Earth's admin 0 data with support for dependent territories:\n\n## Natural Earth Admin 0 Data Downloads\n\nTo view dependent overseas regions broken out (like in ISO codes, such as France), you should use map units instead of the standard countries dataset.\n\n### Available Formats\n\nNatural Earth offers all vector themes available for download as SHP (shapefiles), SQLite, or GeoPackage formats.\n\n### Data Structure\n\nAuxiliary themes include 298 cartographic map units and 360 subunits optimized for labeling edge cases, like the four constituent countries of England, Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland in the United Kingdom. Countries distinguish between metropolitan (homeland) and independent and semi-independent portions of sovereign states.\n\n### Available Scales\n\nThe data is available at multiple scales including 1:10m, 1:50m, and 1:110m resolutions, with each scale having different levels of detail suitable for various mapping purposes.\n\nFor specific downloads and access to the complete datasets in shapefile or GeoJSON formats, you can visit the Natural Earth downloads page at naturalearthdata.com.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"e027189d-89ee-4adb-8af2-893cf5f13bad","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:16.478Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"eb5f6a64-77c0-40da-b7a6-6cf580eeaece","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Harvard Dataverse\",\"url\":\"https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataset.xhtml?persistentId=doi:10.7910/DVN/T9SDEW\"},{\"title\":\"Introducing COLDAT: The Colonial Dates Dataset | Request PDF\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/347417517_Introducing_COLDAT_The_Colonial_Dates_Dataset\"},{\"title\":\"Introducing COLDAT: The Colonial Dates Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://osf.io/preprints/apvqm/\"},{\"title\":\"(PDF) Introducing COLDAT: The Colonial Dates Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://www.academia.edu/85578563/Introducing_COLDAT_The_Colonial_Dates_Dataset\"},{\"title\":\"Bastian Becker - Our World in Data\",\"url\":\"https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/european-overseas-colonies-and-their-last-colonizer\"},{\"title\":\"SOCIUM • SFB 1342 WorkingPapers No. 2 Bastian Becker Introducing COLDAT:\",\"url\":\"https://www.socium.uni-bremen.de/f/51ebd445c4.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"European overseas colonies by colonizer - Our World in Data\",\"url\":\"https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/european-overseas-colonies-by-colonizer\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about COLDAT:\n\n## Colonial Dates Dataset (COLDAT) Overview\n\nThe Colonial Dates Dataset (COLDAT) aggregates information on the reach and duration of European colonial empires from renowned secondary sources. COLDAT provides information on the colonial power and the start and end date of colonial rule.\n\nBy aggregating secondary sources, rather than collecting from primary sources, the new dataset reflects the accumulated knowledge in the discipline and relieves researchers from making hard to justify choices between different historical datasets.\n\n## Key Features\n\n- The dates in COLDAT are expressed as (1) the mean dates over all datasets and (2) the latest dates of all datasets.\n- The dataset only considers the colonial status of countries that are independent today. It also includes overseas colonies only.\n\n## Availability\n\nThe dataset is available through Harvard Dataverse, as indicated by the persistent identifier (DOI: 10.7910/DVN/T9SDEW).\n\nHowever, based on the search results, I should note that while COLDAT contains temporal and categorical data about colonial territories, the search results do not specifically mention polygon shapefiles or GIS-formatted boundary files. If you need geographic boundary data in GIS format for colonial territories, you may need to check the full dataset documentation or supplementary materials directly on the Harvard Dataverse page.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"03d33758-7e32-4c51-8d17-057ec7b99f5a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:17.090Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"2e1830bb-3a2f-404c-9af6-823487dec87a","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

The Centennia Research Edition (CRE) provides extensive GIS versions of Centennia's primary historical database and has been developed for academic research, with institutional licensing fees applying.\n\nThe Centennia Nations Edition covering 1789-1939 is available for free by download or on USB. To access the full time period from 1000 AD to present, you will need to purchase an access code, and all data are included in the download.\n\n## GeaCron Historical Atlas\n\nThe search results show that GeaCron provides an \"Interactive World History Atlas since 3000 BC,\" but it offers no possibility to extract polygons for use in a GIS. A commercial version of the program allows you to see a timeline, though direct GIS data downloads don't appear to be available in the same way as Centennia.\n\nFor GIS-compatible historical boundary data, the search results also reference a GitHub collection of georeferenced boundaries of world countries and cultural regions for use in mapping historical data as an alternative resource.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"9612510d-cebd-444e-8016-f716cadbb434","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:19.898Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"f75ff198-0e7b-440a-8a34-fdcfe1236987","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"19ef5e2f-1cad-434a-92ad-9b5fc0aecdd4","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XZotKGDuwT2cSQzPWcuVuT","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Wikidata SPARQL query historical territory polygon boundary geoshape\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Wikidata:SPARQL query service/queries/examples - Wikidata\",\"url\":\"https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Wikidata:SPARQL_query_service/queries/examples\"},{\"title\":\"Wikidata:Map data - Wikidata\",\"url\":\"https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Wikidata:Map_data\"},{\"title\":\"SPARQL/Views - Wikibooks, open books for an open world\",\"url\":\"https://en.wikibooks.org/wiki/SPARQL/Views\"},{\"title\":\"Wikidata:SPARQL query service/queries - Wikidata\",\"url\":\"https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Wikidata:SPARQL_query_service/queries\"},{\"title\":\"Wikidata:SPARQL query service - Wikidata\",\"url\":\"https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Wikidata:SPARQL_query_service\"},{\"title\":\"A Guide to using SPARQL Queries — Playing with Wikidata | by Swapnil Sinha | Medium\",\"url\":\"https://medium.com/@swaapniil/a-guide-to-using-sparql-queries-playing-with-wikidata-cc5e2f6a5ffc\"},{\"title\":\"T204045 Support GeoSPARQL in Wikidata Query Service\",\"url\":\"https://phabricator.wikimedia.org/T204045\"},{\"title\":\"Wikidata:SPARQL tutorial - Wikidata\",\"url\":\"https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Wikidata:SPARQL_tutorial\"},{\"title\":\"Querying Wikidata with SPARQL – Loretta C. Duckworth Scholars Studio\",\"url\":\"https://sites.temple.edu/tudsc/2021/12/15/querying-wikidata/\"},{\"title\":\"ways to use SPARQL to query Wikidata\",\"url\":\"https://sparql.dev/article/ways_to_use_SPARQL_to_query_Wikidata.html\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about using Wikidata SPARQL queries to work with historical territory polygon boundaries and geoshapes:\n\n## Key Information\n\nMap data is geographical information stored as points, lines or polygons on a map which describes a physical area. It can store information for current and historical political boundaries, geological boundaries, species distribution maps etc and Earth, other planets and even fictional places.\n\n## Using Geoshape Property\n\nTo add a map data file to a Wikidata item, use geoshape (P3896) and add the name of the map data file in Wikimedia Commons as the value.\n\n## Example Query Syntax\n\nA geoshapes query example uses SELECT DISTINCT to retrieve countries, their labels, and geoshapes, where the country is defined as an instance of Q3624078, and then retrieves the geoshape property (P3896).\n\n## Working with Historical Data\n\nFor historical territories, you would use Wikidata's statement and qualifier system. Using start time and end time qualifiers allows you to track temporal information about entities. This enables you to query historical boundaries by filtering for specific time periods.\n\n## File Formats\n\nPolygon data can be available in other formats, including .KML, .TopoJSON, and .CSV. These formats require conversion to GeoJSON before being added to Wikimedia Commons.\n\nThe Wikidata Query Service allows you to visualize these results using the `#defaultView:Map` syntax to display geospatial data on an interactive map.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"014daee5-1870-4063-bf63-de098f657587","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:20.839Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"19ef5e2f-1cad-434a-92ad-9b5fc0aecdd4","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"59367110-f56a-4cf6-b94c-98d0f183ed1f","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01S2pbKp2zZHryjdp76oWBZm","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Harvard WorldMap CGA historical territory layers GIS download\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Harvard Library - Harvard Geospatial Library\",\"url\":\"https://library.harvard.edu/services-tools/harvard-geospatial-library\"},{\"title\":\"Using Harvard WorldMap on ArcGIS Online\",\"url\":\"https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/2542dc5664264765a9fbd889fc71156b\"},{\"title\":\"Publications | Center for Geographic Analysis, Harvard University\",\"url\":\"http://gis2.harvard.edu/publications\"},{\"title\":\"CGA-Legacy\",\"url\":\"https://maps.cga.harvard.edu/cga-legacy/page/14/\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - cga-harvard/worldmap: WorldMap GeoNode Project\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/cga-harvard/worldmap\"},{\"title\":\"Harvard Center for Geographic Analysis - Harvard University\",\"url\":\"https://gis.harvard.edu/\"},{\"title\":\"WorldMap - Harvard University\",\"url\":\"https://worldmap.harvard.edu/\"},{\"title\":\"China Historical GIS | CGA-Legacy\",\"url\":\"https://maps.cga.harvard.edu/cga-legacy/2015/03/20/2015-03-20-china-historical-gis/\"},{\"title\":\"WorldMap - Harvard Center for Geographic Analysis\",\"url\":\"https://gis.harvard.edu/projects/worldmap\"},{\"title\":\"Harvard WorldMap | SoCalGIS.org\",\"url\":\"https://socalgis.org/2018/10/09/harvard-worldmap/\"}]\n\nBased on the web search results, I found information about Harvard WorldMap and the Center for Geographic Analysis (CGA):\n\n## Harvard WorldMap Overview\n\nWorldMap is a collaborative mapping project based at the Harvard Center for Geographic Analysis. Harvard University's Center for Geographic Analysis (CGA) has created Harvard WorldMap, an online open-source mapping platform developed to lower barriers for scholars who wish to explore, visualize, edit, and publish geospatial information.\n\n## Key Features\n\nAnyone can create their own online mapping portal, upload large GIS layers and overlay them with other layers, and control access to their data. In 2021 WorldMap was moved from GeoNode to ArcGIS Online (AGOL) to improve performance and opportunities for collaboration.\n\n## Historical GIS Capabilities\n\nThe search results indicate that WorldMap and the CGA support historical GIS work. For example, the CHGIS project has produced a comprehensive series of datasets related to the administrative geography of Chinese History, with data layers including nationwide coverages for the years 1820 and 1911.\n\nFor specific information about accessing and downloading historical territory layers from Harvard WorldMap, you may want to visit the main WorldMap platform or contact the Harvard Center for Geographic Analysis directly, as the search results don't provide detailed information about specific download procedures for historical territory data.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"b6ad6b6f-4894-4787-91f1-551e33b53562","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:32.789Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"59367110-f56a-4cf6-b94c-98d0f183ed1f","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"cfa5089e-eaaa-4ec0-b5ff-b1fb493b80c9","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_014uYwLUHyxLi5qLvore9MWq","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"OpenHistoricalMap OpenStreetMap historical boundaries project\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"OpenHistoricalMap/Projects - OpenStreetMap Wiki\",\"url\":\"https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/OpenHistoricalMap/Projects\"},{\"title\":\"OpenHistoricalMap - Wikipedia\",\"url\":\"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OpenHistoricalMap\"},{\"title\":\"OpenHistoricalMap | OpenStreetMap US\",\"url\":\"https://openstreetmap.us/tags/openhistoricalmap/\"},{\"title\":\"OpenHistoricalMap/FAQ - OpenStreetMap Wiki\",\"url\":\"https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/OpenHistoricalMap/FAQ\"},{\"title\":\"OpenHistoricalMap - OpenStreetMap Wiki\",\"url\":\"https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Open_Historical_Map\"},{\"title\":\"OpenHistoricalMap/Sources – OpenStreetMap Wiki\",\"url\":\"https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/OpenHistoricalMap/Sources\"},{\"title\":\"OpenHistoricalMap/Contributing - OpenStreetMap Wiki\",\"url\":\"https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/OpenHistoricalMap/Contributing\"},{\"title\":\"OpenHistoricalMap/Projects/Newberry Atlas of Historical County Boundaries Import - OpenStreetMap Wiki\",\"url\":\"https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/OpenHistoricalMap/Projects/Newberry_Atlas_of_Historical_County_Boundaries_Import\"},{\"title\":\"OpenHistoricalMap/Resources - OpenStreetMap Wiki\",\"url\":\"https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/OpenHistoricalMap/Resources\"},{\"title\":\"OpenHistoricalMap/Plans - OpenStreetMap Wiki\",\"url\":\"https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/OpenHistoricalMap/Plans\"}]\n\nI've completed the web search on OpenHistoricalMap and the OpenStreetMap historical boundaries project. Here's a summary of what I found:\n\n## Overview\n\nOpenHistoricalMap is an online collaborative mapping project developing a historical map of the world using OpenStreetMap technology and processes. Whereas OpenStreetMap only includes data about the present day and deletes data as it becomes outdated, OpenHistoricalMap welcomes historical data and preserves multiple copies of a feature as it changes over time.\n\n## Key Features\n\nOpenHistoricalMap tries to gather data that does not belong in the OpenStreetMap database but still should be on some map, with typical examples being historical railway tracks that have been disassembled. OpenHistoricalMap's addition of a time dimension makes the data more complex, and tags for indicating a feature's start and end dates are much more important.\n\n## Historical Boundaries Projects\n\nMajor contributions have included historical boundaries of U.S. states and counties, imported from the Atlas of Historical County Boundaries, a project of the William M. Scholl Center for American History and Culture at the Newberry Library. The Newberry Library's Atlas of Historical County Boundaries includes ~17.7K shapes of county and county equivalents, covering the ~4K county and county equivalents that have existed throughout the country's history.\n\n## Community Organization\n\nOpenHistoricalMap is informally organized into community projects that focus on specific places or themes in the map across time, and these projects are documented to coordinate efforts and serve as a starting point for research.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"2be7f312-5023-4007-8f62-d39b34e36272","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:32.792Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"cfa5089e-eaaa-4ec0-b5ff-b1fb493b80c9","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

historical maps from your specified time period:\n\n**USGS Historical Topographic Map Collection:**\n- The HTMC contains over 185,000 high-resolution, georeferenced digital files with accompanying metadata\n- These scanned quadrangle maps are georeferenced at various scales, dating from the late 1880's to 2010\n- The National Map Downloader is the primary portal for finding and downloading {"parentUuid":"96d95929-4a41-4a41-ac20-c69a45ecbccf","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01S7N3SmUY6oG5YjDynqgDUz","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"David Rumsey Map Collection georeferenced historical maps download GIS\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"David Rumsey Historical Map Collection | Two Dimensional GIS Browser\",\"url\":\"https://www.davidrumsey.com/view/2d-gis\"},{\"title\":\"David Rumsey Historical Map Collection | Georeferencer\",\"url\":\"https://www.davidrumsey.com/view/georeferencer\"},{\"title\":\"David Rumsey Historical Map Collection | View Collection\",\"url\":\"https://www.davidrumsey.com/view\"},{\"title\":\"David Rumsey Historical Map Collection | The Collection\",\"url\":\"https://www.davidrumsey.com/\"},{\"title\":\"David Rumsey Historical Map Collection | Georeferenced Maps\",\"url\":\"https://www.davidrumsey.com/view/georeferenced-maps\"},{\"title\":\"David Rumsey Historical Map Collection\",\"url\":\"https://rumsey.geogarage.com/\"},{\"title\":\"David Rumsey Historical Map Collection - Databases - UW-Madison Libraries\",\"url\":\"https://search.library.wisc.edu/database/UWI12247\"},{\"title\":\"David Rumsey Historical Map Collection | Georeferencer Help\",\"url\":\"https://www.davidrumsey.com/view/georeferencer-help\"},{\"title\":\"David Rumsey Historical Map Collection | Google Maps\",\"url\":\"https://www.davidrumsey.com/view/google-maps\"},{\"title\":\"David Rumsey Historical Map Collection | All Categories\",\"url\":\"https://www.davidrumsey.com/blog\"}]\n\nI found comprehensive information about the David Rumsey Map Collection and its georeferenced historical maps. Here are the key findings:\n\n## Overview\n\nThe historical map collection has over 145,000 maps and related images online. The collection includes rare 16th through 21st century maps of North America, South America, Europe, Asia, Africa, Australia, Antarctica, the Pacific, the Arctic, and the World.\n\n## GIS Browser and Download Options\n\nResults of user customized map layer visualizations can be saved and downloaded as new images, complete with the georeferencing information, thereby allowing easy integration into other desktop GIS applications. The David Rumsey GIS Viewer allows online visitors to easily interact with, integrate, and visualize historic maps along with modern geospatial data from NASA, USGS, ESRI and GCS Research. The modern geospatial data that can be overlaid and compared to the historical maps includes urban areas, transportation infrastructure (roads, rail), public land survey, lakes, parks, state boundaries, digital elevation models and satellite imagery.\n\n## Georeferencer Tool\n\nThe Georeference window allows you to georeference maps, view maps in 2D and 3D, geoedit maps, get map links for GIS apps, and transcribe annotations on the maps. Georeferencing is done using a GIS program, which takes points on the old maps (cities, coast lines, rivers, streets) and connects them to the same points on a modern satellite map image. The GIS program then takes all these points (as many as 200 are made for very large maps) and uses them to recreate the digital image so it will fit into its modern geographical space.\n\n## Google Maps and Earth Integration\n\nOver 120 historical maps in Google Maps have been selected by David Rumsey from his collection of more than 150,000 historical maps. 120 maps can be viewed in the Rumsey Historical Map layer in the Gallery Layer, or you can download links to those maps plus another 140 maps and put them in My Places in Google Earth.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"3ce3f2b3-d516-41cf-bbda-3d361ea8ced4","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:37.337Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"96d95929-4a41-4a41-ac20-c69a45ecbccf","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

UC Berkeley GeoData Search Results\",\"url\":\"https://geodata.lib.berkeley.edu/?f%5Bdct_spatial_sm%5D%5B%5D=Pacific+Ocean\"},{\"title\":\"Boundary, Dominican Republic, 2015 | Stanford Digital Repository\",\"url\":\"https://purl.stanford.edu/zn830hx1848\"},{\"title\":\"Stanford Geospatial Center\",\"url\":\"https://stanford.maps.arcgis.com/\"},{\"title\":\"Boundary, Qatar, 2015 | Stanford Digital Repository\",\"url\":\"https://purl.stanford.edu/hq104py8439\"}]\n\nBased on my search results, here's what I found about Stanford Digital Repository and historical boundary shapefiles:\n\n## Stanford Digital Repository and Geospatial Data\n\nStanford University Libraries EarthWorks provides digitized maps that can be downloaded as shapefiles. Publicly accessible and downloadable shapefiles are available under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International license.\n\nThe Stanford Digital Repository contains various boundary shapefiles. For example, the repository includes polygon shapefiles containing administrative boundaries as part of the Global Administrative Areas 2015 (v2.8) dataset. These datasets are freely available for academic use and other non-commercial use.\n\nStanford also maintains a Geospatial Center that houses historical geospatial resources and maps. Beyond current administrative boundaries, Stanford's collections include historical map materials that have been georeferenced for GIS analysis.\n\nHowever, my search results did not return specific information about Princeton's geospatial historical boundaries collections or direct comparisons between Stanford and Princeton resources in this area. {"parentUuid":"0a20d14d-9996-4f6c-b9a1-5b9db0230b83","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01TGyXshVtrERKKJBb5gm7ni","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"IPUMS NHGIS international historical boundary shapefiles GIS\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"GIS Files | IPUMS NHGIS\",\"url\":\"https://www.nhgis.org/gis-files\"},{\"title\":\"IPUMS NHGIS | National Historical Geographic Information System\",\"url\":\"https://www.nhgis.org/\"},{\"title\":\"Geography & GIS | IPUMS IHGIS\",\"url\":\"https://ihgis.ipums.org/geography-gis\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping Options | IPUMS NHGIS\",\"url\":\"https://www.nhgis.org/mapping/mapping-options\"},{\"title\":\"IPUMS IHGIS | International Historical Geographic Information System\",\"url\":\"https://ihgis.ipums.org/\"},{\"title\":\"NHGIS - IPUMS Forum\",\"url\":\"https://forum.ipums.org/c/nhgis/26\"},{\"title\":\"Data | IPUMS NHGIS\",\"url\":\"https://www.nhgis.org/data\"},{\"title\":\"IPUMS USA | GIS Boundary Files\",\"url\":\"https://usa.ipums.org/usa/volii/boundaries.shtml\"},{\"title\":\"GIS Files | IPUMS IHGIS\",\"url\":\"https://ihgis.ipums.org/documentation/gis-data\"},{\"title\":\"Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) | IPUMS NHGIS\",\"url\":\"https://www.nhgis.org/frequently-asked-questions-faq\"}]\n\nHere are the search results for \"IPUMS NHGIS international historical boundary shapefiles GIS\":\n\n## Key Findings:\n\n**IPUMS NHGIS (U.S. Data):**\nNHGIS provides its geometry files for geographic information systems (GIS) as shapefiles, a standard spatial data file format. The National Historical Geographic Information System provides data with GIS-compatible mapping files, for years from 1790 through the present and for all levels of U.S. census geography, including states, counties, tracts, and blocks.\n\n**IPUMS IHGIS (International Data):**\nThe International Historical Geographic Information System (IHGIS) provides data tables from population and housing censuses as well as agricultural censuses from around the world, along with corresponding GIS boundary files. IHGIS provides shapefiles to describe the boundaries of administrative and statistical units represented in IHGIS data files. By linking the data tables to the boundary files, you can map the characteristics in the data tables. The GISJOIN field present in both the shapefiles and the data files serves as the link between the files.\n\n**Boundary File Organization:**\nA shapefile is provided for each geographic level for which data are tabulated. Geographic levels are numbered with g0 being the entire nation, g1 being the largest sub-national units, g2 being subdivisions of g1 units, and so on. Typically, g1 represents the first administrative level (e.g., states or provinces), g2 represents the second administrative level (e.g., counties, districts, or municipalities), and so on.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"1c07a7a0-afd1-4e8e-b50c-e329f37491c3","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:49.867Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"0a20d14d-9996-4f6c-b9a1-5b9db0230b83","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"9a5ae052-ceda-400b-adcc-ef62fb69dbbd","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016pCmzVXyjoj8rjg1588GAr","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"colonial territory GIS polygons shapefile dataset \"colonial boundaries\" historical download\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - Guy Schvitz, Luc Girardin, Seraina Rüegger, Nils B. Weidmann, Lars-Erik Cederman, Kristian Skrede Gleditsch, 2022\",\"url\":\"https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/full/10.1177/00220027211013563\"},{\"title\":\"Finding GIS Data: US - Maps & Geospatial: Geographic Information Systems (GIS) - Library Guides at Penn State University\",\"url\":\"https://guides.libraries.psu.edu/c.php?g=376207&p=5296082\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - aourednik/historical-basemaps: Collection of georeferenced boundaries of world countries and cultural regions for use in mapping historical data on global or continental scale · GitHub\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/aourednik/historical-basemaps\"},{\"title\":\"Cartographic Boundary Files - Shapefile\",\"url\":\"https://www.census.gov/geographies/mapping-files/time-series/geo/carto-boundary-file.html\"},{\"title\":\"Where to Find GIS Data for Historical Country Boundaries - Geography Realm\",\"url\":\"https://www.geographyrealm.com/find-gis-data-historical-country-boundaries/\"},{\"title\":\"Download data by country | DIVA-GIS\",\"url\":\"https://www.diva-gis.org/gdata\"},{\"title\":\"About the Project | Atlas of Historical County Boundaries Project\",\"url\":\"https://publications.newberry.org/ahcb/project.html\"},{\"title\":\"U.S. Historical Counties\",\"url\":\"https://publications.newberry.org/ahcb/documents/US_HistCounties_Metadata2.htm\"},{\"title\":\"Download Files | Atlas of Historical County Boundaries Project\",\"url\":\"https://publications.newberry.org/ahcb/downloads/\"},{\"title\":\"GIS Data Download | U.S. Geological Survey\",\"url\":\"https://www.usgs.gov/the-national-map-data-delivery/gis-data-download\"}]\n\nI found several relevant resources for colonial territory GIS polygon shapefile datasets with historical boundaries. Here are the key findings:\n\n## Primary Resources:\n\n**CShapes 2.0 Dataset**\nCShapes 2.0 is a GIS dataset that maps the borders of states and dependent territories from 1886 through 2019. The dataset maps the borders of colonies and other dependencies, providing near complete global coverage of political units throughout recent history. The dataset represents states and dependencies as GIS polygons. CShapes 2.0 is freely available for academic and other non-commercial purposes.\n\n**HGIS de las Indias**\nThe HGIS de las Indias site features thousands of boundary files covering colonial Spanish America from 1701–1808, with more than one thousand base maps available for smaller provinces and jurisdictions in three moments: 1701, 1750 and 1800.\n\n**Historical-Basemaps Project**\nThe historical boundaries project aims at providing ready-to-use base maps for mapping historical data, though the project is work in progress and users should verify maps by comparison to other sources before using in academic work.\n\n## Additional Resources:\n\n- The Atlas of Historical County Boundaries provides shapefiles covering historical boundaries for the entire US, created in 2010 by the William M. Scholl Center for American History and Culture at The Newberry Library in Chicago.\n\n- The Pleiades project collects historical geographical information for the Greek and Roman world and other regions, with data available in CSV, JSON, and KML formats.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"6c904856-2a05-453a-887f-ea70f4bdb870","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:51.944Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"9a5ae052-ceda-400b-adcc-ef62fb69dbbd","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"7fe960ed-2a9f-450e-8edb-1f7293d84407","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QqcPajBxX2c2TCJpthsxMV","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Max Planck MPIDR Census Mosaic historical boundaries shapefiles Europe coverage years\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Historical GIS datafiles | Censusmosaic\",\"url\":\"https://censusmosaic.demog.berkeley.edu/data/historical-gis-files\"},{\"title\":\"Mosaic data files | Census Mosaic\",\"url\":\"https://mosaic.ipums.org/mosaic-data-files\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS datafiles | Census Mosaic\",\"url\":\"https://mosaic.ipums.org/historical-gis-datafiles\"},{\"title\":\"Austro_Hungarian_Empire_1910_v_1_0 (FeatureServer)\",\"url\":\"https://services8.arcgis.com/UN2BoTelitQIJWcd/ArcGIS/rest/services/Austro_Hungarian_Empire_1910_v_1_0/FeatureServer\"},{\"title\":\"(PDF) Mosaic: recovering surviving census records and reconstructing the familial history of Europe\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/273508116_Mosaic_recovering_surviving_census_records_and_reconstructing_the_familial_history_of_Europe\"},{\"title\":\"Data by Location: International - Geographic Information Systems & Cartography - Subject Guides at American University\",\"url\":\"https://subjectguides.library.american.edu/c.php?g=175021&p=1155762\"},{\"title\":\"Find DATA & Shapefiles - GIS: Geographic Information Systems - LibGuides at Northwestern University\",\"url\":\"http://libguides.northwestern.edu/c.php?g=115072&p=749535\"},{\"title\":\"Mosaic data files | Censusmosaic\",\"url\":\"https://censusmosaic.demog.berkeley.edu/data/mosaic-data-files\"},{\"title\":\"Full article: Mosaic: recovering surviving census records and reconstructing the familial history of Europe\",\"url\":\"https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/1081602X.2015.1006655\"},{\"title\":\"Mosaic project | European Historical Population Samples Network\",\"url\":\"https://ehps-net.eu/databases/mosaic-project\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about the Max Planck MPIDR Census Mosaic historical boundaries shapefiles:\n\n## Overview\nThe MPIDR Population History GIS Collection supports demographic and socioeconomic research by filling gaps in the European GIS-data-infrastructure on historical national and regional administrative boundaries and historical place names.\n\n## Geographic Coverage\nThere are GIS files with administrative boundaries available for Albania, Austria-Hungary, Courland, Germany, Poland and Serbia, and maps for the whole of Europe with the borders in 1900, 1930, 1960, 1990 and 2003.\n\n## Specific Shapefiles Available\nThe collection includes several key historical datasets:\n\n- **Austro-Hungarian Empire (1910)**: Providing information on the administrative division of the Austro-Hungarian Empire at the level of the court districts in 1910. This shapefile has been created by Helmut Rumpler and Martin Seger and been slightly modified for the MPIDR Population History GIS Collection.\n\n- **Albania (1918)**: Providing information on the administrative division of Albania at the level of the municipalities in 1918. The shapefile only covers the northern and central part of present-day Albania, which had been occupied by the Austro-Hungarian Empire during World War I.\n\n- **Serbia**: Providing information on the administrative division of Serbia at the level of districts (srez). This shapefile-series has been created by Siegfried Gruber and geo-referenced by Heidi Harris for the MPIDR Population History GIS Collection.\n\n## Years Covered\nProviding information on the administrative division of the European states at varying geographic detail in 30-years intervals (1900, 1930, 1960, 1990, 2003).\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"c14c88bc-29a0-485e-973e-3abd3ce116f6","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:08:52.390Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"7fe960ed-2a9f-450e-8edb-1f7293d84407","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

EarthWorks\",\"url\":\"https://earthworks.stanford.edu/catalog/stanford-tj316jq1243\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about Euratlas historical boundaries shapefiles:\n\n## Coverage and Data Scope\n\nEuratlas maps cover the Earth's surface from 15 degrees west to 50 degrees east and 20 degrees north to 60 degrees north, describing the political situation at the first day of each centennial year from year 1 to 2000. The maps have been improved and redrawn in 2008 and are available as shapefiles data which can be analyzed with GIS software.\n\n## Format and Data Layers\n\nData is provided in platform-independent shapefile format and requires Geographic Information System (GIS) software such as Open Jump, Map Window or Arc GIS. Each century includes layers for autonomous peoples, cities with name and size information, rivers, seas, lakes, fuzzy borders, and mountains provided as five contour line layers.\n\n## Academic Access\n\nFor academic institutions, there are multiple access paths:\n\n1. Duke University provides Euratlas Historical Political Boundaries data to current Duke University students, faculty and staff.\n\n2. Stanford Libraries offers these data to Stanford University affiliates (faculty, staff and students), with non-affiliates able to contact the publisher directly.\n\n3. The data is also available for purchase directly from the Euratlas shop website, though specific pricing information was not included in the search results.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"c568849e-ea1c-4ceb-9304-4a3294206fd5","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:09:02.304Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"856add57-b085-4ad5-b6c9-863504225be2","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

For instance, there is a file for year 1492 (world_1492.geojson), and I can see references to files like world_bc2000.geojson and world_700.geojson in the repository structure, indicating coverage spanning from ancient times to recent history.\n\nHowever, the search results do not provide a complete comprehensive list of all available years. The Thenmap API can fetch geodata as GeoJSON or TopoJSON, prerendered maps as SVG files, or metadata.\n\n## Coverage and Data Format\nThenmap is an API and JavaScript library that allows you to create maps with historical map borders, and you can download historical map borders in formats including TopoJSON and GeoJSON.\n\n## Usage\nTo make a map of Finnish municipalities on January 1 2016, you can use a URL like http://api.thenmap.net/v2/fi-8/geo/2016-01-01?geo_props=name&language=fi. You replace the dataset id (like fi-8) with the dataset you want to use, the language code with your language of choice, and the date as required.\n\nThe search results indicate that Thenmap focuses on historical boundaries with specific dataset coverage, though the complete list of all countries and year ranges covered isn't fully detailed in these results.
