{"parentUuid":"f80bd5cc-a3f0-4634-bcc3-688e67c1f999","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_015MwQcnpHPBonKb28y48sWh","type":"tool_result","content":"# GeoJSON Files in the Directory\n\nBased on the GitHub repository content, here are all the `world_YEAR.geojson` files available:\n\n**Ancient/BCE files (Before Common Era):**\nworld_bc123000, world_bc10000, world_bc8000, world_bc5000, world_bc4000, world_bc3000, world_bc2000, world_bc1500, world_bc1000, world_bc700, world_bc500, world_bc400, world_bc323, world_bc300, world_bc200, world_bc100, world_bc1\n\n**CE/Common Era files:**\n100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700, 800, 900, 1000, 1100, 1200, 1279, 1300, 1400, 1492, 1500, 1530, 1600, 1650, 1700, 1715, 1783, 1800, 1815, 1880, 1900, 1914, 1920, 1930, 1938, 1945, 1960, 1994, 2000, 2010\n\n**Additional file:**\nplaces.geojson\n\nThis collection provides historical world basemap data spanning from 123,000 years BCE through 2010 CE."}]},"uuid":"1e1b22e3-402c-4a86-b323-098e63dcc497","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:10:11.770Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"f80bd5cc-a3f0-4634-bcc3-688e67c1f999","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"0a91720c-ea42-4fbd-b142-bcf2d94121f5","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XdvsxJgsHRJXExjoFYNRrA","type":"tool_result","content":"# Admin 0 Countries Dataset Overview\n\n## What's Included\n\nThe Admin 0 Countries dataset contains **258 countries** and distinguishes between metropolitan territories and independent/semi-independent portions of sovereign states. Notably, \"Greenland as separate from Denmark\" is included.\n\nThe documentation clarifies an important distinction: this file focuses on sovereign states rather than dependent territories. As stated, \"Most users will want this file instead of sovereign states, though some users will want map units instead when needing to distinguish overseas regions of France.\"\n\n## What's Excluded\n\nDependent territories and overseas colonies are **not** included in the standard download. For these subdivisions, users are directed to the \"map units\" alternative dataset instead.\n\n## Available Formats\n\nThe downloads are provided in multiple formats:\n- Shapefile\n- GeoDB\n- TIFF format\n\n## Data Included\n\nEach country entry contains:\n- ISO and FIPS codes\n- French INSEE codes\n- World region classification (following UN methodology)\n- Thematic data sourced from the United Nations, U.S. CIA, and other organizations\n\n## Boundary Representation\n\nThe dataset shows \"de facto boundaries by default according to who controls the territory,\" with optional point-of-view variants available for several dozen countries."}]},"uuid":"49101f29-29bf-4e6c-9e8e-cccd0c4f19b1","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:10:12.305Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"0a91720c-ea42-4fbd-b142-bcf2d94121f5","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"4408a5a5-eb96-48e3-87ea-8d9d7ef324f9","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0187NGLiV3YNuUiqHTYCfstR","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"HGIS de las Indias colonial Spanish America GIS boundary shapefile download\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Data: Territorial gazetteer for Spanish America, 1701-1808 - HGIS de las Indias\",\"url\":\"https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataset.xhtml?persistentId=doi:10.7910/DVN/YPEU5E\"},{\"title\":\"HGIS de las Indias\",\"url\":\"https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataverse/hgis-indias\"},{\"title\":\"Actividades y publicaciones\",\"url\":\"https://www.hgis-indias.net/index.php/documentacion/actividades-y-publicaciones\"},{\"title\":\"HGIS de las Indias – digital humanities im deutschsprachigen raum\",\"url\":\"https://digitalhumanities.de/en/projekt/hgis-de-las-indias/\"},{\"title\":\"Where to Find GIS Data for Historical Country Boundaries - Geography Realm\",\"url\":\"https://www.geographyrealm.com/find-gis-data-historical-country-boundaries/\"},{\"title\":\"Data: Post Offices of Spanish America, 1765-1808 - HGIS de las Indias\",\"url\":\"https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataset.xhtml;jsessionid=6b7a4f63c1584f34cf4c78175faf?persistentId=doi%3A10.7910%2FDVN%2F8SDFTT&version=&q=&fileTypeGroupFacet=&fileAccess=\"},{\"title\":\"Digital Resources: HGIS de las Indias | Oxford Research Encyclopedia of Latin American History\",\"url\":\"https://oxfordre.com/latinamericanhistory/display/10.1093/acrefore/9780199366439.001.0001/acrefore-9780199366439-e-822\"},{\"title\":\"Digital Mapping for Historians and Humanists - GIS & Mapping - Guides at Penn Libraries\",\"url\":\"https://guides.library.upenn.edu/c.php?g=1321452&p=9877060\"},{\"title\":\"HGIS de las Indias | Economic Growth Center\",\"url\":\"https://egc.yale.edu/bridging-atlantic-migrations-and-their-legacies/resources\"},{\"title\":\"HGIS of Indias, a historic SIG of colonial America - GEHC\",\"url\":\"https://www.ub.edu/gehc/en/hgis-of-indias-a-historic-sig-of-colonial-america/\"}]\n\nBased on the web search results, here's what I found about HGIS de las Indias:\n\n## Overview\nHGIS de las Indias is an open-access Spanish-language database and web platform on the temporal and spatial developments in the territorial organization and settlements of all Spanish America during the reign of the Bourbon dynasty until the eve of the independence movements (1701–1808).\n\n## Data Available\nThe project offers shapefiles and geopackages with information on territorial organization of the Spanish Empire in America, 1701-1808, organized in 11 levels including principal divisions, audiencias, intendencies, provinces, jurisdictions, districts, fiefdoms, archbishoprics, bishoprics, and frontiers/uncontrolled areas. The database contains over 15,000 settlements and over 1,000 administrative units with their qualitative, spatial, and temporal attributes.\n\n## Where to Download\nAll data from HGIS de las Indias are accessible under a Creative Commons 4.0 license at the Harvard Dataverse. The primary data repository is at: https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataverse/hgis-indias\n\n## Additional Features\nThe project consists of several components: a platform for visualization of the database in an interactive web application, an engine for the creation of base maps, and a repository for the raw data files that can be used in specialized software.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"ba3ee337-d86b-404d-8d50-7ebe342c3d89","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:10:14.893Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"4408a5a5-eb96-48e3-87ea-8d9d7ef324f9","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"e159f9aa-833f-4758-911b-5bd1f49a73a4","isSidechain":true,"agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"progress","data":{"type":"search_results_received","resultCount":10,"query":"Colonialism in Global Perspective dataset GIS shapefile"},"toolUseID":"srvtoolu_01VdXambmB1gy2ZnzZuDBiSA","parentToolUseID":"toolu_01GXKbHFS2K4WhRTmZS4cmxK","uuid":"408e3242-fee2-45c3-9c25-633f99dfcb6d","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:10:23.152Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"ca1feeed-f383-4d7b-be4e-661921ebaa7d","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UptRe76faekSAvcgzuG5N1","type":"tool_result","content":"# Admin 0 Details Layer Overview\n\n## Coverage Summary\n\nThe Admin 0 Details layer includes **209 sovereign states** globally. It provides four distinct datasets with varying levels of political subdivision detail.\n\n## Dataset Components\n\n**Sovereignty Layer:** Contains 209 sovereign states that issue passports, excluding those that don't (10 states).\n\n**Map Units:** Encompasses \"298 cartographic map units\" including groups like the four constituent countries within the United Kingdom (England, Scotland, Wales, Northern Ireland).\n\n**Map Subunits:** Covers countries divided into non-contiguous geographic regions, such as Alaska separated from the continental United States.\n\n**Scale Ranks:** Breaks multipart polygons into individual units with assigned scale rankings, exemplified by Indonesia's island subdivisions.\n\n## Political Entity Types\n\nThe layer covers:\n- Sovereign states\n- Dependencies and leases\n- Constituent countries\n- Overseas and island territories\n- Cartographic units for labeling purposes\n- Non-contiguous subunits\n\nThe dataset explicitly acknowledges dependencies, territories, and semi-independent portions of states through its comprehensive classification system, though the specific count of dependent territories isn't numerically specified in the documentation."}]},"uuid":"0e29499d-3b03-4ca3-9e5c-6d7fbad64573","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:10:24.440Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"ca1feeed-f383-4d7b-be4e-661921ebaa7d","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"2da83070-b156-41f9-b2dd-aa60f5b3579b","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016Gg5fFsHmmoqAUmfxbaeP2","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"CShapes 2.0 R package cshapes dependent territories list number colonies how many dependencies\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - CShapes\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/cshapes/\"},{\"title\":\"The CShapes 2.0 Dataset and Utilities\",\"url\":\"https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/cshapes/cshapes.pdf\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping and Measuring Country Shapes\",\"url\":\"https://journal.r-project.org/articles/RJ-2010-004/index.html\"},{\"title\":\"Cshapes - Earth Observer\",\"url\":\"https://www.earth.observer/datasets/cshapes/\"},{\"title\":\"cshapes source: R/cshp.R\",\"url\":\"https://rdrr.io/cran/cshapes/src/R/cshp.R\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - Guy Schvitz, Luc Girardin, Seraina Rüegger, Nils B. Weidmann, Lars-Erik Cederman, Kristian Skrede Gleditsch, 2022\",\"url\":\"https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/full/10.1177/00220027211013563\"},{\"title\":\"ETH - International Conflict Research - R Package for CShapes\",\"url\":\"https://icr.ethz.ch/data/cshapes/r-package/\"},{\"title\":\"cshp function - Access the CShapes dataset in R\",\"url\":\"https://www.rdocumentation.org/packages/cshapes/versions/2.0/topics/cshp\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - Research Repository\",\"url\":\"https://repository.essex.ac.uk/31791/\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://ideas.repec.org/a/sae/jocore/v66y2022i1p144-161.html\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, I found information about CShapes 2.0 and dependent territories, but the results don't provide a specific count of how many dependent territories or colonies are included in the dataset. Here's what I found:\n\nCShapes 2.0 maps the borders and capitals of independent states and dependent territories from 1886 to 2019. The new dataset is no longer limited to independent states, but also maps the borders of colonies and other dependent territories, thus providing near complete global coverage throughout recent history.\n\nIn the R package, there is a Boolean argument specifying whether dependent territories must be included - TRUE returns polygons for both independent states and dependent units, while FALSE returns polygons for independent states only (default).\n\nOne illustrative example is that whereas the previous version only contains four polygons for Africa in 1946, CShapes 2.0 covers all fifty-four states and dependencies on the continent.\n\nHowever, the search results do not provide the total number of dependent territories or colonies included in the CShapes 2.0 dataset. You may need to consult the full dataset documentation or the accompanying research paper for a complete inventory of all dependent territories covered.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"ce9fa561-7091-491a-be68-a7b8acb906fd","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:10:24.659Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"2da83070-b156-41f9-b2dd-aa60f5b3579b","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"e159f9aa-833f-4758-911b-5bd1f49a73a4","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Ja7VokkPUhS6ErCBMFn9bV","type":"tool_result","content":"# Thenmap Datasets Overview\n\n## Available Datasets\n\nAccording to the documentation, Thenmap provides historical border data in these specific formats:\n\n**Geographic Data:**\n- \"GeoJSON or TopoJSON, prerendered maps as SVG files, or metadata\"\n\n**Coverage by Region:**\n\nThe API holds data for:\n- World borders (1945–)\n- Swedish municipalities (1974–) and counties (1968–)\n- Finnish municipalities (2010–)\n- US states (1865–)\n- Norwegian municipalities (2006–) and counties (2018+)\n- Municipalities of Greenland (1979–)\n- Municipalities of Switzerland (2010–)\n- Danish municipalities (2017+)\n\n## Data Formats\n\nThe system provides multiple output options:\n1. **GeoJSON/TopoJSON** - Vector geographic data\n2. **SVG files** - Pre-rendered map graphics\n3. **Metadata** - Information sourced from WikiData, including details like historical currencies\n\n## Access Methods\n\nUsers can retrieve data through the Thenmap API by \"passing a date and optionally a preferred coordinate system or projection,\" along with JavaScript library integration and a Python library for programmatic access."}]},"uuid":"399b7a84-d0eb-443f-8da4-f47351aab57b","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:10:26.311Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"e159f9aa-833f-4758-911b-5bd1f49a73a4","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"56450382-0e8c-4c34-a3ef-51bca59730fe","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01GXKbHFS2K4WhRTmZS4cmxK","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"\"Colonialism in Global Perspective\" dataset GIS colonial territory shapefile\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset - Guy Schvitz, Luc Girardin, Seraina Rüegger, Nils B. Weidmann, Lars-Erik Cederman, Kristian Skrede Gleditsch, 2022\",\"url\":\"https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/full/10.1177/00220027211013563\"},{\"title\":\"Research Note: Measuring the Impacts of Colonialism: A New Data Set for the Countries of Africa and Asia | Journal of World-Systems Research\",\"url\":\"https://jwsr.pitt.edu/ojs/jwsr/article/view/683\"},{\"title\":\"Global - International GIS Data - Guides at Penn Libraries\",\"url\":\"https://guides.library.upenn.edu/globalgis\"},{\"title\":\"Using GIS to Map Settler Colonialism in Pennsylvania – Loretta C. Duckworth Scholars Studio\",\"url\":\"https://sites.temple.edu/tudsc/2021/05/14/using-gis-to-map-settler-colonialism-in-pennsylvania/\"},{\"title\":\"Data by Location: International - Geographic Information Systems & Cartography - Subject Guides at American University\",\"url\":\"https://subjectguides.library.american.edu/c.php?g=175021&p=1155762\"},{\"title\":\"Download data by country | DIVA-GIS\",\"url\":\"https://www.diva-gis.org/gdata\"},{\"title\":\"Colonialism in Open Data and Mapping | Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team\",\"url\":\"https://www.hotosm.org/updates/colonialism-in-open-data-and-mapping/\"},{\"title\":\"QoG Data\",\"url\":\"https://datafinder.qog.gu.se/variable/ht_colonial\"},{\"title\":\"GIS Datasets - Data Sources & Repositories - Research Guides at Cal Poly, San Luis Obispo\",\"url\":\"https://guides.lib.calpoly.edu/c.php?g=261997&p=1750196\"},{\"title\":\"(De)Colonial historical geography and historical GIS - ScienceDirect\",\"url\":\"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0305748822000846\"}]\n\nBased on my search results, I found some relevant information about GIS datasets related to colonialism:\n\nThe most relevant resource I found is CShapes 2.0, a GIS dataset that maps the borders of states and dependent territories from 1886 through 2019, and also maps the borders of colonies and other dependencies, thereby providing near complete global coverage of political units throughout recent history. The dataset extends temporal coverage back to the year 1886, which followed the Berlin Conference on the partition of Africa.\n\nAdditionally, I found reference to a dataset with 15 indicators for the political, economic and social impact of colonialism, which creates the opportunity to compare the levels of colonial transformation for a sample of 83 African and Asian countries.\n\nAnother resource worth noting is a web map representing indigenous lands throughout the world, which is created to foster conversation about the history of colonialism and Indigenous ways of knowing.\n\nFor accessing GIS shapefiles more broadly, you may also want to explore repositories like DIVA-GIS, which stores vector data as ESRI shapefiles—a commonly used format that can be directly used in Arc-anything, DIVA-GIS, and many other programs, and can be imported to most other GIS programs.\n\nIf you're looking for a specific dataset called \"Colonialism in Global Perspective\" with colonial territory shapefiles, you may want to search university library GIS repositories or academic data repositories directly, as these results show related resources but not necessarily a dataset by that exact title.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"065b77b9-6e56-4bb5-8199-e46d1d588c9c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:10:28.435Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"56450382-0e8c-4c34-a3ef-51bca59730fe","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

vLex United States - vLex\",\"url\":\"https://law-journals-books.vlex.com/vid/mapping-the-international-system-879944959\"},{\"title\":\"The CShapes 2.0 Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://d-nb.info/125016981X/34\"},{\"title\":\"Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset | Request PDF\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/342342479_Mapping_the_International_System_1886-2019_The_CShapes_20_Dataset\"},{\"title\":\"(PDF) Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/351339723_Mapping_the_International_System_1886-2019_The_CShapes_20_Dataset\"},{\"title\":\"[PDF] Mapping the International System, 1886-2019: The CShapes 2.0 Dataset | Semantic Scholar\",\"url\":\"https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/Mapping-the-International-System%2C-1886-2019%3A-The-Schvitz-Girardin/b466260bb7b70157f7557baf7af29f317965ab49\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here is information about the CShapes 2.0 paper abstract:\n\nCShapes 2.0 is a GIS dataset that maps the borders of states and dependent territories from 1886 through 2019. The dataset is no longer limited to independent states, but also maps the borders of colonies and other dependencies, thereby providing near complete global coverage of political units throughout recent history.\n\nThe two main improvements over the previous version include:\n\n1. It extends temporal coverage from 1946 back to the year 1886, which followed the Berlin Conference on the partition of Africa.\n\n2. The new dataset is no longer limited to independent states, but also maps the borders of colonies and other dependencies, thereby providing near complete global coverage of political units throughout recent history.\n\nRegarding the coverage of dependent territories, the coding of dependent territories includes the following four categories from the original COW list: (1) colonies, (2) protectorates, (3) international mandates and (4) occupied territories.\n\nThe paper demonstrates these improvements by showing that whereas the previous version only contains four polygons for Africa in 1946, CShapes 2.0 covers all fifty-four states and dependencies on the continent.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"5c0e1223-69a0-47b4-bff5-4513f47ac5e4","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:10:37.652Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"679bc1c5-ce54-4739-8804-5175e6bcfb34","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

- General - OpenHistoricalMap Forum\",\"url\":\"https://forum.openhistoricalmap.org/t/how-to-download-roman-empire-borders-from-openhistoricalmap-for-different-periods/541\"},{\"title\":\"Boundary viewer demo - General - OpenHistoricalMap Forum\",\"url\":\"https://forum.openhistoricalmap.org/t/boundary-viewer-demo/695\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's information about OpenHistoricalMap and downloading boundary data:\n\n## Overview of OpenHistoricalMap\n\nOpenHistoricalMap is an interactive map of the world throughout history, created by people like you and dedicated to the public domain. It is an online collaborative mapping project developing a historical map of the world using OpenStreetMap technology and processes, and whereas OpenStreetMap only includes data about the present day, OpenHistoricalMap welcomes historical data and preserves multiple copies of a feature as it changes over time.\n\n## Downloading and Extracting Boundary Data\n\nFor accessing boundary data from OpenHistoricalMap:\n\n- OpenHistoricalMap's main REST API allows you to upload and download raw data in OSM XML format, which is generally only useful to developers, but if you have a node, way, or relation ID and simply need to look up its raw tags, this would be the fastest and most up-to-date method.\n\n- Each element's page contains a Download XML link to the raw element data in OSM XML format, which is compatible with QGIS, and for relations such as boundary and chronology relations, you should use the /full method to recursively download the relation and its members.\n\n- For more advanced visualizations that require desktop processing, OpenHistoricalMap/QGIS can load OpenHistoricalMap tiles and stylesheets directly, or it can query the Overpass API for more selective data processing.\n\n## Colonial Territories and Coverage\n\nOne of OpenHistoricalMap's projects includes \"Colonizing Trails West\" documenting routes of the mass migration west. However, the search results indicate that OpenHistoricalMap does not currently have a regional extract service.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"9c553e15-6746-4942-8531-c0e185efcfd2","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:10:43.829Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"a4b009d1-c4cc-432c-89ec-bb60fd55f021","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

Countries and Territories — Huwise\",\"url\":\"https://public.opendatasoft.com/explore/dataset/world-administrative-boundaries/export/?flg=en-us\"},{\"title\":\"Global - International GIS Data - Guides at Penn Libraries\",\"url\":\"https://guides.library.upenn.edu/globalgis\"},{\"title\":\"WorldMap / Dataverse Integration | Center for Geographic Analysis\",\"url\":\"https://gis.harvard.edu/worldmap-dataverse-integration\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here are several resources available through Harvard Dataverse for historical political boundaries and GIS shapefiles:\n\n## Key Resources on Harvard Dataverse\n\nHarvard DataVerse hosts historical GIS data, with several notable datasets:\n\n1. **geoBoundaries Global Administrative Database**: A static version of the geoBoundaries database has been deposited on the Harvard Dataverse. This is an online, open license (CC BY 4.0) resource of information on administrative boundaries for every country in the world.\n\n2. **China and Japan Historical Data**: There is a free database of place names and historical administrative units for the Chinese Dynasties available via Harvard Dataverse. Similarly, a free database of place names and historical administrative units for the Japan Dynasties is available via Dataverse at Harvard.\n\n3. **Russian Empire Data**: Imperiia: A Spatial History of the Russian Empire (Harvard Dataverse) is a project documenting the spatial history of the Russian Empire and includes historical administrative boundaries, biodiversity, and migration data.\n\n4. **Additional Historical Datasets**: Skinner Regional Systems Analysis Dataverse includes French cantons from 1806-1896, plus miscellaneous local data on China and Japan.\n\nThese resources provide shapefiles and other GIS-compatible formats for historical political boundaries at global, regional, and country-specific scales.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"0ce85f69-f970-4d9f-9f37-33de63e05c6e","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:10:45.085Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"2cbf8b57-d9ff-4d65-b147-771de3492f39","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

The search results indicate that the dataset includes data files in CSV, Shapefile, and GeoJSON formats. However, I did not find explicit mention of GeoPackage format in the search results for CShapes 2.0, though it is a common modern GIS format.\n\n## Data Structure and Polygons\n\nThe dataset represents states and dependencies as GIS polygons. The dataset uses a multi-polygon representation where a set of polygons is represented by a record in the attribute table, allowing countries like Indonesia to correspond to exactly one record despite consisting of 121 disconnected polygons.\n\n## Coverage\n\nCShapes 2.0 maps the borders and capitals of independent states and dependent territories from 1886 to 2019.\n\nI was unable to find the specific total number of entities or polygons in the complete CShapes 2.0 dataset from these search results. For that specific information, you may need to consult the official CShapes documentation at ETH Zurich's International Conflict Research website or download the dataset to count the features directly.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"ac60c964-3401-45ef-8fe4-9797205b50b9","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:10:59.510Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"0fcb9ca0-5be1-4156-843a-8e30e542b09e","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

aourednik/historical-basemaps: Collection of georeferenced boundaries of world countries and cultural regions for use in mapping historical data on global or continental scale · GitHub\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/aourednik/historical-basemaps\"},{\"title\":\"From Empire to Nation-State: Explaining Wars in the Modern World, 1816-2001\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/275491279_From_Empire_to_Nation-State_Explaining_Wars_in_the_Modern_World_1816-2001\"},{\"title\":\"From Empire to Nation-State: Explaining Wars in the Modern World, 1816–2001 - Andreas Wimmer, Brian Min, 2006\",\"url\":\"https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/000312240607100601\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS datafiles | Censusmosaic\",\"url\":\"https://censusmosaic.demog.berkeley.edu/data/historical-gis-files\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about the Wimmer and Min \"From Empire to Nation-State\" dataset:\n\n## Dataset Overview\n\nThe \"From Empire to Nation-State\" dataset takes fixed geographical territories instead of countries as units of analysis, enabling the tracing of a territory's political and economic development before and after independence from 1816 to 2001, and is the replication dataset for Wimmer and Min's 2006 American Sociological Review article \"From empire to nation-state: Explaining war in the modern world, 1816-2001\".\n\n## Content and Coverage\n\nThe dataset contains variables indicating economic, political, and cultural modernization processes on the world's territories before they transition to the nation-state, the presence of nationalist organizations, wars, dependency from imperial centers, the power of these centers, the years in which a territory transitions to the nation-state, and covers 140 territories (in their post-independence geographic extension) from 1816 onward.\n\n## Access and Download\n\nThe data files and the codebook are downloadable at Brian Min's website by clicking \"From Empire to Nation-State\" on the left column.\n\n## Note on GIS Format\n\nThe search results indicate that this is primarily a statistical dataset (available in formats like Stata and Excel) rather than a GIS-specific geographic dataset with spatial boundaries. If you're specifically looking for GIS-compatible historical territorial boundaries, you may want to explore complementary resources like the historical-basemaps project mentioned in the results, which provides georeferenced boundaries in GeoJSON format.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"f81a9c5d-f4ae-4cba-9234-a179d0ee22cc","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:11:01.485Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"3588d08c-cd12-44f5-8947-552925589bf6","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"607a9c2a-dca3-4a12-a5d1-17af43b9ea87","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01D9cFP8vddRyctH3cCLFCus","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Centennia Research Edition CRE historical vector data price download shapefile GIS academic license\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"Centennia Research Edition (CRE).\",\"url\":\"http://historicalatlas.com/CRE/\"},{\"title\":\"HistoricalAtlas.com: The CENTENNIA Historical Atlas, Europe and the Middle East, 1000AD to the Present\",\"url\":\"http://historicalatlas.com/editions/\"},{\"title\":\"Download data by country | DIVA-GIS\",\"url\":\"https://www.diva-gis.org/gdata\"},{\"title\":\"Free GIS Data\",\"url\":\"https://freegisdata.rtwilson.com/\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS Data and Maps - Humanities GIS - InfoGuides at George Mason University\",\"url\":\"https://infoguides.gmu.edu/geohumanities/data\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS Resources Thread | alternatehistory.com\",\"url\":\"https://www.alternatehistory.com/forum/threads/historical-gis-resources-thread.516971/\"},{\"title\":\"Euratlas Shop: Georeferenced Historical Vector Data 1300\",\"url\":\"https://www.euratlas.net/shop/maps_gis/gis_1300.html\"},{\"title\":\"Euratlas Shop: Georeferenced Historical Vector Data 1500\",\"url\":\"https://www.euratlas.net/shop/maps_gis/gis_1500.html\"},{\"title\":\"Download Listing Data - GIS Shapefiles | Historic England\",\"url\":\"https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/data-downloads/\"},{\"title\":\"Historical - GIS Data Sources - LibGuides at Duke University\",\"url\":\"https://guides.library.duke.edu/gisdata/gisdata_historical\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about Centennia Research Edition (CRE):\n\n## Overview\nCentennia Research Edition (CRE) has been designed for professional academic research and analysis by historians, political scientists, and economists requiring basemap data for political entities in Europe and the Middle East during the past ten centuries.\n\n## Data Format and Compatibility\nThe data is in standard KML format compatible with all modern GIS, with latitude/longitude rectification, and includes polygons and identifiers at all dates. The maps may be imported into any modern GIS analysis system.\n\n## Pricing Options\n\nCRE offers several licensing tiers:\n\n- CRE Single-date: A complete map for any single date in the period from 1000AD-2003 is priced at $75 with a signed contract.\n\n- CRE 1789-1939: The KML mapping data has a license fee of $3,125 (signed contract required).\n\n- CRE Complete: The complete KML dataset covering 1000AD-2003, with signed license required, is priced at $12,500.\n\n## Licensing Requirements\nAll licenses of Centennia Research Edition (CRE) require a signed licensing contract and include limitations on publication, display, and other uses. Institutional licensing fees apply.\n\nTo purchase a license of CRE, you need to contact them directly.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"490773f9-bd72-4d3e-be24-1bf4256da860","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:11:03.797Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"607a9c2a-dca3-4a12-a5d1-17af43b9ea87","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

awesome-gee-community-catalog\",\"url\":\"https://gee-community-catalog.org/projects/geoboundary/\"},{\"title\":\"GitHub - wmgeolab/geoBoundaries: geoBoundaries : A Political Administrative Boundaries Dataset (www.geoboundaries.org)\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/wmgeolab/geoBoundaries\"},{\"title\":\"Comprehensive Global Administrative Zones (CGAZ)\",\"url\":\"https://www.geoboundaries.org/globalDownloads.html\"},{\"title\":\"(PDF) geoBoundaries: A global database of political administrative boundaries\",\"url\":\"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/340924081_geoBoundaries_A_global_database_of_political_administrative_boundaries\"},{\"title\":\"Administrative Boundaries - PubMed\",\"url\":\"https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32330167/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about geoBoundaries and accessing historical versions:\n\n## Overview of geoBoundaries\n\ngeoBoundaries is produced and maintained by the William & Mary geoLab since 2017 and is an online, open license resource of boundaries for every country in the world. geoBoundaries datasets are provided under the CC BY 4.0 license, which allows for most commercial, noncommercial, and academic uses.\n\n## Download Methods\n\nThree different mechanisms are provided to download geoBoundaries data: a web-based GUI, HTML file access on GitHub (for archival versions), and an API.\n\n## Accessing Historical Versions\n\nFor historical versions, the search results indicate that:\n\n- A static version is archived on the Harvard Dataverse.\n- HTML file access on GitHub is available for archival versions.\n- A URL structure can be used to construct download links for any file in the database—for example, https://geoboundaries.org/data/geoBoundaries-2_0_0/JPN/ADM0/geoBoundaries-2_0_0-JPN-ADM0-shp.zip can be used to download the shapefile for the specified country and ADM level.\n\n## Primary Resources\n\nThe main resources for accessing geoBoundaries are:\n- Official website: www.geoboundaries.org\n- GitHub repository: https://github.com/wmgeolab/geoBoundaries\n- Harvard Dataverse for archived versions\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"b3cf0d35-aecd-45f1-815f-04f0346a64af","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:11:16.088Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"d1988333-43f9-449b-bc58-0d08225891e1","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

(FAQ) | IPUMS IHGIS\",\"url\":\"https://ihgis.ipums.org/frequently-asked-questions-faq\"},{\"title\":\"GIS Files | IPUMS NHGIS\",\"url\":\"https://www.nhgis.org/gis-files\"},{\"title\":\"GIS Files | IPUMS IHGIS\",\"url\":\"https://ihgis.ipums.org/documentation/gis-data\"}]\n\nBased on the web search results, here's what I found about IHGIS and the availability of boundary shapefiles:\n\n## Overview\n\nThe International Historical Geographic Information System (IHGIS) provides data tables from population and housing censuses as well as agricultural censuses from around the world, along with corresponding GIS boundary files.\n\n## Boundary Shapefiles\n\nIHGIS provides shapefiles to describe the boundaries of administrative and statistical units represented in IHGIS data files. A shapefile is provided for each geographic level for which data are tabulated, with geographic levels numbered with g0 being the entire nation, g1 being the largest sub-national units, g2 being subdivisions of g1 units, and so on.\n\n## Data Sources and Coverage\n\nThe GIS boundary files draw on a variety of data sources, with attempts to obtain source shapefiles from the country's national statistical office or geographic agency, though most often boundary data in an international data source is used as a starting point, with common sources including GADM, the UN Second Administrative Level Boundaries (SALB) project, and the Food and Agriculture Organization's Global Administrative Unit Layers (GAUL).\n\nA summary of the datasets available in IHGIS can be found on the Dataset Descriptions page, with data for many more datasets at various stages in the processing pipeline.\n\n## Technical Details\n\nIHGIS shapefiles are in the WGS 1984 geographic coordinate system. The GISJOIN field present in both the shapefiles and the data files serves as the link between the files.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"78a32ab7-ac35-4e71-851a-f57ae976ef4c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:11:17.328Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"a6a32233-4dab-45ac-be06-f107c37c4f3d","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

The latest version of the dataset and associated reference documents can be downloaded free of charge, with each ZIP including the Dataset, Codebook, What's New, Cautionary Notes, and Suggested Citations. You can access these downloads through the official V-Dem website at www.v-dem.net/data.\n\nAdditionally, previous dataset versions can be accessed, with external archives available at the SND and at CurateND where e.g., posterior distributions for variables, and older versions (v1-v7) can be accessed.\n\n## Related GIS Polygon Boundary Data\n\nWhile the search results did not reveal a dedicated V-Dem GIS polygon boundaries dataset, they identified related resources for historical polity boundaries:\n\n- Historical country boundaries (2000 BCE to 1994 CE) and Euratlas Georeferenced Historical Vector Data covering years 100, 500, 1000, 1200, 1400, 1600, 1800, and 2000 A.D are available through institutional access.\n\n- The geoBoundaries Global Database of Political Administrative Boundaries is an online, open license (CC BY 4.0) resource, with all boundaries available to view or download in common file formats.\n\nFor the most current information about V-Dem's specific GIS polygon offerings, I recommend visiting www.v-dem.net directly or contacting their support team.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"b95578a9-9fd1-42c0-b2a5-556a0e5e0830","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:11:19.613Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"3cd98241-d18b-492e-9e33-af988819acfe","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

NYU Spatial Data Repository\",\"url\":\"https://geo.nyu.edu/catalog/harvard-euratlas-svrgn-states-700\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about Euratlas:\n\n## Overview\n\nEuratlas Historical Political Boundaries data describe the political situation in Europe at the turn of each century A.D. The map covers Europe from 15 degrees west to 50 degrees east, 20 degrees north to 60 degrees north.\n\n## Data Format and Layers\n\nThe data is provided in platform independent shapefile format. Each century consists of several layers including: Autonomous peoples, Cities with name and size information, Dioceses/eyalets or regions, Sovereign_countries (all independent states or sovereign entities with dependencies included in their territory), Holder_countries (all entities with dependent or not, dependent territories not included in sovereign territories), and Provinces (second-level administrative divisions).\n\n## Coverage\n\nThe Euratlas collection is composed of 21 maps, one for each century from year 1 to year 2000, depicting the detailed political situation at the first day of each centennial year.\n\n## Purchase and Access\n\nWhen purchased, users receive a web-link valid 1 week with login and password to download the data as a ZIP file. However, the search results do not contain specific pricing information per century.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"c73a2a0a-9be0-4d29-b7bf-3d0689cd4042","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:11:29.491Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"95a32d4c-9b98-4560-9dc0-2feced496b8f","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"058b79ec-78e0-4538-9c9c-3a70c15a8817","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0181QbM3UnqVfq7qZPtUBJkQ","type":"tool_result","content":"# Euratlas 1900 Historical Vector Data\n\n**Pricing & Licenses:**\n- Simple license: 150 € + VAT\n- Extended license: 600 € + VAT\n- Site license: starting from 600 € for 10,000 users\n\n**Geographic Coverage:**\nThe dataset covers \"the Earth's surface from the degree 15 west to 50 east, and from degree 20 north to 60 north,\" depicting Europe's political situation as of the year 1900.\n\n**Format:**\nShapefile format (platform-independent), requiring GIS software like Open Jump, Map Window, or Arc GIS.\n\n**Included Entities:**\nThe data contains multiple layers:\n- Autonomous peoples (populations without defined authority)\n- Cities (with name and size information)\n- Dioceses/eyalets (second-level administrative divisions)\n- Sovereign countries (independent states)\n- \"Holder\\_countries: all entities, dependent or not, dependent territories not being included in the sovereign territories\"\n- Provinces (smallest administrative units)\n- Physical features (rivers, seas, mountains)\n\n**Regarding Dependencies:**\nYes, the dataset includes dependencies. It specifically notes that dependent territories are tracked separately—\"dependencies (=holder countries) being included in their territory\" for sovereign states, while holder countries include \"dependent or not\" entities."}]},"uuid":"f05e1f09-3973-4bd2-990c-94133a51422a","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:11:30.003Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"058b79ec-78e0-4538-9c9c-3a70c15a8817","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

The project is run by Harvard's Yenching Institute.\n\n## Coverage and Data\n\nThe data layers include nationwide coverages for the years 1820 and 1911, and time series for the Dynastic period from 221 BCE to 1911 CE. The administrative features include Provinces, Circuits, Prefectures, and Counties as they changed over time. Included in this dataset are also more recent shapefiles of roads, rivers, railways and even contours.\n\n## Download Location\n\nThe distribution URL is https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataverse/chgis_v6, and data can be downloaded via Harvard Dataverse.\n\n## License and Access\n\nAll CHGIS data is published for free academic use. Commercial use of this data is possible but you will need to obtain a license from the CHGIS Management Committee.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"11fc055d-2271-478a-bfa9-040a55cede22","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:11:33.126Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"8b0b142d-0fa8-4bcc-8032-588370238507","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

This is a component of the Imperiia Project.\n\n## GIS Boundary Shapefiles\n\nThe spatial data generated through the project is published in the Imperiia Dataverse. Each spatial dataset is provided as a zipped shapefile and is accompanied by a metadata document with usage notes and attribute definitions.\n\nAdditionally, some spatial datasets can be downloaded directly from the Imperiia Map (where they appear as map layers). The WorldMap platform allows layer downloads in the following formats: zipped shapefile, GML 2.0, GML 3.1.1, CSV, Excel, GeoJSON, GeoTIFF, JPEG, PDF, PNG, KML.\n\n## Data Available\n\nThe Imperiia: A Spatial History of the Russian Empire project in Harvard Dataverse documents the spatial history of the Russian Empire and includes historical administrative boundaries, biodiversity, migration data, etc.\n\nThe main Harvard Dataverse collection can be accessed at: https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataverse/ImperiiaGIS\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"df245d9c-a6d7-4d97-94c6-b535d12b28ab","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:11:33.442Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"8cc132a7-22d8-4e4d-8ea4-e9731f77cf56","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

GIS Data - Research Guides at Brandeis University\",\"url\":\"https://guides.library.brandeis.edu/c.php?g=990410&p=7166514\"},{\"title\":\"Where to Find GIS Data for Historical Country Boundaries - Geography Realm\",\"url\":\"https://www.geographyrealm.com/find-gis-data-historical-country-boundaries/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, here's what I found about Princeton's geospatial data resources:\n\n## Princeton's Geospatial Data Resources\n\nPrinceton's Digital Maps and Geospatial Data Portal (PULMap) provides users the opportunity to discover a selection of datasets and scanned historical maps from their collections, as well as the collections of other partner institutions. Patrons may easily search for and browse online content in a variety of ways, including a user-friendly spatial search, to find relevant data on a particular location on earth.\n\nAn extensive collection of GIS data is available through the Map and Geospatial Information Center. While much of the content is freely available in their portal, some maps and datasets are restricted to Princeton University-affiliated patrons or \"on-site only\" use.\n\nFor assistance with finding specific historical boundaries data or shapefiles, you can contact Tsering Wangyal Shawa, GIS and Map Librarian.\n\nThe search results indicate that Princeton maintains geospatial resources, but I didn't find specific details about a world historical boundaries shapefile available for download. For access to specific historical boundary data in shapefile format, you would likely need to consult directly with Princeton's Map and Geospatial Information Center or browse their PULMap portal at maps.princeton.edu.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"5b110b2c-3ada-474d-9078-f270cb2a51eb","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:11:48.874Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"0fbd6a4d-1dd4-4e5f-bafe-05ef873d4841","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

GIS (Geographic Information Systems) & Remote Sensing - Library Guides at UC Berkeley\",\"url\":\"https://guides.lib.berkeley.edu/gis/international\"},{\"title\":\"Data by Location: International - Geographic Information Systems & Cartography - Subject Guides at American University\",\"url\":\"https://subjectguides.library.american.edu/c.php?g=175021&p=1155762\"},{\"title\":\"Mosaic data files | Censusmosaic\",\"url\":\"https://censusmosaic.demog.berkeley.edu/data/mosaic-data-files\"},{\"title\":\"Mosaic project | European Historical Population Samples Network\",\"url\":\"https://ehps-net.eu/databases/mosaic-project\"},{\"title\":\"Historical GIS Resources Thread | alternatehistory.com\",\"url\":\"https://www.alternatehistory.com/forum/threads/historical-gis-resources-thread.516971/\"}]\n\nBased on the search results, I found relevant information about the MPIDR Census Mosaic historical GIS resources you're looking for.\n\n## MPIDR Population History GIS Collection\n\nThe MPIDR Population History GIS Collection supports demographic and socioeconomic research by filling gaps in the European GIS-data-infrastructure on historical national and regional administrative boundaries and historical place names. The data is available through multiple platforms:\n\n### Access Points\n\nThe data can be accessed through:\n- **IPUMS Mosaic**: https://mosaic.ipums.org/historical-gis-datafiles\n- **CensusMosaic at UC Berkeley**: https://censusmosaic.demog.berkeley.edu/data/historical-gis-files\n\n### European Administrative Boundaries Shapefile\n\nThe collection provides information on the administrative division of the European states at varying geographic detail in 30-years intervals (1900, 1930, 1960, 1990, 2003). Until 1960, geocodes are based on the IDs used in the Princeton European Fertility Project.\n\n### Download Terms\n\nThe data provided can be freely used for non-commercial scientific purposes, as long as you register as a user with the Mosaic project and the source of the data is properly cited.\n\nThe shapefile downloads are available in ZIP format with detailed citation information included in the files themselves.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"ffba8b05-ba52-4d86-a842-1f1426018fc2","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:12:00.177Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"a0b3c103-0a22-41ab-b881-2d48744d8399","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}

{"parentUuid":"a1b31136-a0a1-4387-a724-beb9b6cd7521","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01RScj1qAkgQrurz9grG6C5u","type":"tool_result","content":"# Available Historical GIS Datafiles\n\n| Name | Geographic Coverage | Time Period | Format | Boundary Details |\n|------|-------------------|------------|--------|-----------------|\n| Austro-Hungarian Empire | Austria-Hungary | 1910 | ZIP | Court districts |\n| FRG and GDR | Germany (divided) | 1949-1989 | ZIP | District level, annual |\n| German Empire | Germany | 1871-1945 | ZIP | Varying state detail, annual |\n| German Empire Statistical Units | Germany | 1871-1931 | ZIP | Small administrative areas (kleine Verwaltungsbezirke) |\n| German Union | German territories | 1815-1870 | ZIP | Varying state detail, annual |\n| Germany (post-reunification) | Federal Republic of Germany | 1990-2009 | ZIP | District level, annual |\n| Albania | Northern/central Albania | 1918 | ZIP | Municipality level |\n| Europe | European states | 1900-2003 | ZIP | Varying detail, 30-year intervals |\n| Kurland | Russian Governorate (Latvia) | 1797 | ZIP | Administrative divisions |\n| Poland | Poland/Polish-Lithuania | 1770-1978 | ZIP | Varying detail, multiple years |\n| Serbia | Serbia | 1865-1895 | ZIP | District (srez) level |\n\nAll files are vector shapefiles available for non-commercial scientific use with user registration and proper citation."}]},"uuid":"a3a187c1-7a95-4198-a099-6ffd3ae99113","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:12:10.824Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"a1b31136-a0a1-4387-a724-beb9b6cd7521","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"4a83f7e1-30c7-4724-af65-2ccfa5505cce","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0724ffbb-5b84-4502-984f-4912bb00cd36","agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01VQPp3L2f7KyBK6KGToAxwm","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"Old Maps Online search API download colonial map 1880 1920 Africa Asia georeferenced boundary\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"GitHub - aourednik/historical-basemaps: Collection of georeferenced boundaries of world countries and cultural regions for use in mapping historical data on global or continental scale · GitHub\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/aourednik/historical-basemaps\"},{\"title\":\"OpenHistoricalMap\",\"url\":\"https://www.openhistoricalmap.org/\"},{\"title\":\"Finding Maps - Historical Maps - Guides at Penn Libraries\",\"url\":\"https://guides.library.upenn.edu/c.php?g=476326&p=3256216\"},{\"title\":\"Historical Maps, GIS and Land Records - GIS - Research Guides at Cal Poly, San Luis Obispo\",\"url\":\"https://guides.lib.calpoly.edu/GIS/HGIS\"},{\"title\":\"19th-Century Maps of the Middle East, North Africa, and Central Asia\",\"url\":\"https://www.lib.uchicago.edu/e/collections/maps/middleeast19/\"},{\"title\":\"Old Maps Online\",\"url\":\"https://www.oldmapsonline.org/en\"},{\"title\":\"Explore Africa Historical Map\",\"url\":\"https://www.worldmap1.com/explore-africa-historical-map\"},{\"title\":\"David Rumsey Historical Map Collection | The Collection\",\"url\":\"https://www.davidrumsey.com/\"},{\"title\":\"The new map of Africa (1900-1916); a history of European colonial expansion and colonial diplomacy : Gibbons, Herbert Adams, 1880- [from old catalog] : Free Download, Borrow, and Streaming : Internet Archive\",\"url\":\"https://archive.org/details/newmapofafrica1900gibb\"},{\"title\":\"Africa Historical Map - Guide of the World\",\"url\":\"https://www.guideoftheworld.com/africa-historical-map.html\"}]\n\nHere are the search results for your query about Old Maps Online and colonial maps from 1880-1920:\n\n## Key Resources Found:\n\n**Old Maps Online**\nOld Maps Online is described as \"the easy-to-use getaway to historical maps in libraries around the world.\"\n\n**David Rumsey Historical Map Collection**\nThe David Rumsey Historical Map Collection has over 145,000 maps and related images online, including rare 16th through 21st century maps of North America, South America, Europe, Asia, Africa, Australia, Antarctica, the Pacific, the Arctic, and the World. The MapRank search tool enables geographical searching of the collection by map location and coverage in a Google Map window, with results ranked by coverage.\n\n**Georeferenced Colonial Maps**\nSome historical map collections contain georeferenced maps that can be found by entering the current address, Latitude/Longitude coordinates, or by looking for the location on a modern Google Map.\n\n**Historical Boundary Data**\nThe David Rumsey Map Collection is described as \"the most important historical maps repository\" with over 60,000 ancient maps, some reprojected on interactive globes.\n\n**African Colonial Maps (1880-1920)**\nThe \"16th- Early 20th Century Maps of Africa\" collection contains 113 antique maps of Africa, dating from between the mid-16th to the early 20th century.\n\nThese resources collectively provide access to colonial-era maps for Africa and Asia with various levels of georeferencing and boundary information available.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"e1baecc0-ade8-4c81-9713-8a12070f9f6e","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:12:13.757Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"4a83f7e1-30c7-4724-af65-2ccfa5505cce","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
{"parentUuid":"e1baecc0-ade8-4c81-9713-8a12070f9f6e","isSidechain":true,"agentId":"ac00fb8dbb50ec388","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_014JTm298dKoRmosMCg3pb3A","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the complete analysis.\n\n---\n\nHere is a systematic assessment of all 15 sources, with exact details on coverage, format, cost, and whether they include dependent territories (colonies, protectorates, etc.).\n\n---\n\n## 1. CShapes 2.0\n\n- **URL**: https://icr.ethz.ch/data/cshapes/\n- **Temporal coverage**: 1886-2019\n- **Spatial coverage**: Global (near complete)\n- **Format**: Shapefile, GeoJSON, CSV; also available as R package (`cshapes` on CRAN)\n- **Cost**: FREE\n- **Dependent territories**: **YES -- this is the single most important dataset for your needs.** CShapes 2.0 was explicitly redesigned to include dependent territories. It codes four categories from the COW list: **(1) colonies, (2) protectorates, (3) international mandates, and (4) occupied territories.** The R package has a Boolean argument to include/exclude dependencies. The paper demonstrates that while CShapes 1.0 had only 4 polygons for Africa in 1946, CShapes 2.0 covers all 54 African states and dependencies. It uses both the Gleditsch & Ward and Correlates of War state lists.\n- **Limitation**: Does NOT cover pre-1886. So you will not get Italian pre-unification states (ended 1860-1870), the Papal States (ended 1870), or any territory change before 1886. Entities like British Malaya, French Indochina, Belgian Congo, German East Africa, and Ottoman Empire provinces ARE covered (they existed within the 1886-2019 window). This is your primary source.\n\n## 2. GADM\n\n- **URL**: https://gadm.org/\n- **Temporal coverage**: **Current boundaries only** (version 4.1, with v5 planned Jan 2026)\n- **Spatial coverage**: Global -- 400,276 administrative areas at 6 levels (national to sub-municipal)\n- **Format**: GeoPackage (primary), Shapefile, KMZ, R spatial objects\n- **Cost**: FREE for academic/non-commercial use; redistribution/commercial use requires permission\n- **Dependent territories**: No historical data at all. **Only present-day boundaries.**\n- **Use as proxy**: Modern admin boundaries CAN be used as rough proxies for some historical territories. For instance, modern Malaysian state boundaries approximate Federated/Unfederated Malay States; modern DRC boundaries approximate Belgian Congo; modern Vietnamese provinces approximate regions of French Indochina. However, this requires manual research to know which modern units map to which historical territory, and borders have often changed.\n\n## 3. Natural Earth\n\n- **URL**: https://www.naturalearthdata.com/\n- **Temporal coverage**: **Current boundaries only** (no historical)\n- **Spatial coverage**: Global\n- **Format**: Shapefile, SQLite, GeoPackage; 3 scales (1:10m, 1:50m, 1:110m)\n- **Cost**: FREE (public domain)\n- **Key polygon layers**:\n  - **Admin 0 - Countries**: 258 countries (sovereign + some semi-independent)\n  - **Admin 0 - Map Units**: 298 units -- breaks out overseas departments (e.g., French Guiana separate from France). **This is the layer you want for dependent territories.**\n  - **Admin 0 - Map Subunits**: 360 subunits (e.g., England/Scotland/Wales/NI separate)\n  - **Admin 0 - Sovereignty**: 209 sovereign states\n  - **Admin 0 - Breakaway/Disputed Areas**: ~100 disputed territories (Kashmir, Western Sahara, etc.)\n  - **Admin 1 - States/Provinces**: Sub-national divisions for all countries\n- **Dependent territories**: Yes, current dependent territories (Bermuda, Guam, New Caledonia, etc.) are included in the Map Units and Subunits layers. Does NOT include historical colonies.\n- **Use case**: Excellent as a modern basemap. Useful for getting current small island/territory polygons that other datasets miss.\n\n## 4. Aourednik Historical Basemaps\n\n- **URL**: https://github.com/aourednik/historical-basemaps\n- **Temporal coverage**: BC 123,000 to 2010 CE. **The years most relevant to your 1860-1960 window are**: 1800, 1815, 1880, 1900, 1914, 1920, 1930, 1938, 1945, 1960\n- **Spatial coverage**: Global (world maps)\n- **Format**: GeoJSON (primary), SVG derivatives\n- **Cost**: FREE (GPL-3.0)\n- **Dependent territories**: **YES.** Each feature has a `SUBJECTO` property indicating the colonial power exercising authority. So British Malaya, French Indochina, Belgian Congo, German East Africa, Ottoman provinces are all represented (as colored by their colonial ruler).\n- **Accuracy warning**: The project explicitly states it is **\"work in progress\"** and advises users to **\"verify the maps by comparison to other sources before using in academic work.\"** Border precision varies (rated 1-3 on `BORDERPRECISION` field). These are coarse world-scale basemaps, not research-grade boundary data. Useful for visualization but not for area calculations.\n- **Polities included**: Empires, kingdoms, tribal areas, cultural regions, and colonial territories.\n\n## 5. COLDAT (Colonial Dates Dataset)\n\n- **URL**: https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataset.xhtml?persistentId=doi:10.7910/DVN/T9SDEW\n- **Temporal coverage**: Colonial periods from ~1500s to independence dates\n- **Spatial coverage**: Global (all countries that were colonized)\n- **Format**: **Tabular only (CSV/Stata) -- NO polygons/shapefiles**\n- **Cost**: FREE (Harvard Dataverse)\n- **What it provides**: Colonial power, start/end dates of colonial rule, aggregated from multiple secondary sources. Dates expressed as mean and latest across datasets. Only considers countries that are independent today.\n- **Dependent territories**: Tracks colonial status temporally but provides NO geographic boundaries. You would need to join this to a spatial dataset (e.g., CShapes or GADM) for mapping.\n\n## 6. Centennia Historical Atlas / GeaCron\n\n**Centennia Historical Atlas**:\n- **URL**: http://historicalatlas.com/\n- **Temporal coverage**: 1000 AD to 2003 (Europe & Middle East)\n- **Format**: KML (standard GIS format)\n- **Downloadable data**: YES, through the **Centennia Research Edition (CRE)**\n  - Free \"Nations Edition\" covers **1789-1939** (download or USB)\n  - CRE Single-date: $75\n  - CRE 1789-1939: **$3,125**\n  - CRE Complete (1000-2003): **$12,500**\n  - All require signed licensing contracts\n- **Dependent territories**: Yes, includes dependencies and sovereign entities within Europe and the Middle East. Covers Italian pre-unification states, Papal States, Ottoman provinces.\n\n**GeaCron**:\n- **URL**: http://geacron.com/\n- **Temporal coverage**: 3000 BC to present (global)\n- **Downloadable data**: **NO.** Visualization only. No way to extract polygons for GIS use.\n\n## 7. Wikidata\n\n- **URL**: https://query.wikidata.org/\n- **Relevant property**: `P3896` (geoshape) -- links to GeoJSON/KML files on Wikimedia Commons\n- **Temporal coverage**: Spotty. Some historical entities have geoshapes, many do not.\n- **Format**: GeoJSON via Wikimedia Commons; queryable via SPARQL\n- **Cost**: FREE\n- **Dependent territories**: In theory, Wikidata items exist for entities like British Malaya (Q871091), French Indochina, Belgian Congo, etc. Some have `P3896` geoshape data, but **coverage is highly inconsistent**. Many historical territories lack polygon data entirely. Start/end dates are available as qualifiers on statements.\n- **Practical assessment**: Useful for targeted lookups of specific entities, but NOT a systematic boundary dataset. You cannot reliably get a complete set of colonial territory polygons from Wikidata SPARQL alone.\n\n## 8. OpenHistoricalMap\n\n- **URL**: https://www.openhistoricalmap.org/\n- **Temporal coverage**: All of history (community-contributed)\n- **Spatial coverage**: Wherever volunteers have mapped\n- **Format**: OSM XML; exportable via Overpass API; viewable in QGIS\n- **Cost**: FREE (public domain)\n- **Coverage reality**: **Very uneven.** The best-covered area is US historical county boundaries (imported from the Newberry Library Atlas). Global colonial territory coverage is **sparse and incomplete**. There is no systematic coverage of colonial Africa, Asia, or pre-unification European states.\n- **Dependent territories**: Some exist but coverage is not reliable for academic research. This is a volunteer project with no quality guarantees.\n\n## 9. Harvard WorldMap / Harvard CGA\n\n- **URL**: https://worldmap.harvard.edu/ (now migrated to ArcGIS Online)\n- **Key sub-projects with polygon data**:\n  - **CHGIS (China Historical GIS)**: Administrative boundaries for Chinese dynasties, 221 BCE - 1911 CE. Provinces, circuits, prefectures, counties. FREE. Harvard Dataverse: https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataverse/chgis_v6\n  - **Imperiia Project (Russian Empire)**: Administrative boundaries of the Russian Empire, 1820s. Shapefiles on Harvard Dataverse: https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataverse/ImperiiaGIS. FREE.\n  - **HGIS de las Indias**: Colonial Spanish America territorial organization, 1701-1808. 11 administrative levels, 15,000+ settlements, 1,000+ admin units. Shapefiles/GeoPackages. FREE (CC BY 4.0). Harvard Dataverse: https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataverse/hgis-indias\n- **Dependent territories**: The sub-projects above explicitly cover colonial/imperial territories (Chinese dynastic provinces, Russian imperial boundaries, Spanish colonial America). However, there is no single unified global historical boundary layer.\n\n## 10. David Rumsey Map Collection\n\n- **URL**: https://www.davidrumsey.com/\n- **Coverage**: 145,000+ maps, 16th-21st century, global\n- **Format**: High-resolution scanned images; many **georeferenced** (can be overlaid on modern maps in GIS). Downloadable as GeoTIFFs.\n- **Cost**: FREE to view/download\n- **Georeferencing**: The Georeferencer tool lets you create control points. Over 120 maps integrated with Google Earth. Results exportable with georeferencing metadata.\n- **Dependent territories**: As raster scans, these show whatever the original cartographer depicted -- colonial boundaries, empire borders, etc. You would need to **manually digitize** polygon boundaries from these scans. This is labor-intensive but provides the most detailed historical boundary information for obscure territories.\n- **Best for**: Finding visual reference for territories that lack vector GIS data, then digitizing boundaries in QGIS.\n\n## 11. Old Maps Online\n\n- **URL**: https://www.oldmapsonline.org/\n- **Coverage**: A search portal/aggregator linking to maps in libraries worldwide\n- **For 1860-1960**: Extensive results -- it indexes collections from the Library of Congress, British Library, David Rumsey, national libraries, etc. You search by location and date range.\n- **Format**: Scanned images (not vector GIS data)\n- **Cost**: FREE to search; downloads depend on the hosting institution\n- **Dependent territories**: Shows whatever the original maps depicted. Like David Rumsey, these are raster images requiring manual digitization. No polygon downloads.\n\n## 12. Stanford Digital Repository / Princeton Geospatial\n\n**Stanford (EarthWorks)**:\n- **URL**: https://earthworks.stanford.edu/\n- **Coverage**: Hosts GADM boundaries, Euratlas data (for Stanford affiliates), and various contributed datasets. Searchable spatial catalog.\n- **Historical boundaries**: Available through Euratlas (Stanford affiliate access) and various contributed datasets. Not a primary data source but a useful discovery platform.\n- **Cost**: Most data free; some restricted to Stanford affiliates\n\n**Princeton (PULMap)**:\n- **URL**: https://maps.princeton.edu/\n- **Coverage**: Geospatial data portal with maps and datasets. Some historical materials.\n- **Historical boundaries**: No specific global historical boundary dataset identified. Primarily a discovery/access platform rather than a data producer.\n\n## 13. IPUMS NHGIS / IHGIS\n\n**NHGIS** (National):\n- **URL**: https://www.nhgis.org/\n- **Coverage**: US only, 1790-present. States, counties, tracts, blocks. Not relevant for colonial territories.\n\n**IHGIS** (International):\n- **URL**: https://ihgis.ipums.org/\n- **Coverage**: Census boundaries for ~50 countries, mostly **single recent census years** (e.g., Ghana 2010, Japan 2010, Ireland 1966-1991, Uganda 1991-2009). NOT historical colonial boundaries.\n- **Format**: Shapefiles\n- **Cost**: FREE (registration required)\n- **Dependent territories**: **No.** These are modern census administrative boundaries, not colonial-era territories. Could serve as modern proxies for some territories (like GADM), but that's not their purpose.\n\n## 14. Colonial Territory GIS -- Best Available Options\n\nThere is **no single \"colonial territory GIS\" dataset** that comprehensively covers all colonial polygons. Your best strategy is to combine:\n\n1. **CShapes 2.0** (1886-2019, global, includes colonies/protectorates/mandates) -- PRIMARY SOURCE\n2. **Aourednik Historical Basemaps** (1800-1960, global, coarse but includes colonies) -- SUPPLEMENTARY\n3. **HGIS de las Indias** (1701-1808, Spanish America, detailed) -- SPECIFIC\n4. **Euratlas** (1 CE-2000 CE, Europe/Middle East, paid, includes sovereign + dependent entities)\n5. **Centennia CRE** (1000-2003, Europe/Middle East, paid, includes all political entities)\n\nFor entities you specifically asked about:\n| Territory | Best Source | Coverage |\n|-----------|-----------|----------|\n| British Malaya | CShapes 2.0 | 1886-1957 |\n| French Indochina | CShapes 2.0 | 1886-1954 |\n| Belgian Congo | CShapes 2.0 | 1886-1960 |\n| German East Africa | CShapes 2.0 | 1886-1919 |\n| Ottoman Empire provinces | CShapes 2.0 (whole empire); Euratlas (provinces) | 1886-1920 |\n| Papal States | Euratlas or Centennia CRE (ended 1870, before CShapes) | Pre-1886 |\n| Italian pre-unification states | Euratlas or Centennia CRE (ended 1860, before CShapes) | Pre-1886 |\n\n## 15. Max Planck MPIDR Census Mosaic\n\n- **URL**: https://mosaic.ipums.org/historical-gis-datafiles (also at https://censusmosaic.demog.berkeley.edu/data/historical-gis-files)\n- **Temporal coverage**: Varies by country/region\n- **Spatial coverage**: **Europe only**\n- **Format**: Shapefiles (ZIP)\n- **Cost**: FREE for non-commercial research (registration required)\n- **Available datasets**:\n  - **Europe-wide**: 1900, 1930, 1960, 1990, 2003 (30-year intervals, varying detail)\n  - **Austro-Hungarian Empire**: 1910 (court district level)\n  - **German Union**: 1815-1870 (annual, varying state detail)\n  - **German Empire**: 1871-1945 (annual, district level)\n  - **German Empire Statistical Units**: 1871-1931\n  - **FRG and GDR**: 1949-1989 (annual, district level)\n  - **Germany post-reunification**: 1990-2009 (annual)\n  - **Albania**: 1918 (municipality level, northern/central only)\n  - **Poland**: 1770-1978 (multiple years)\n  - **Serbia**: 1865-1895 (district level)\n  - **Kurland** (Russian governorate, Latvia): 1797\n- **Dependent territories**: Not explicitly colonial territories, but the Austro-Hungarian Empire 1910 dataset and the Europe-wide maps DO show imperial/multinational state boundaries. The Europe 1900 map includes Ottoman European territories. These are census-based administrative boundaries, so they show internal provincial divisions of empires.\n\n---\n\n## Summary Recommendation for Your Project\n\n**For an academic project needing colonial/historical territory polygons, use this tiered approach:**\n\n1. **Start with CShapes 2.0** -- it is FREE, academically rigorous (published in Journal of Conflict Resolution), and covers 1886-2019 with explicit colony/protectorate/mandate polygons globally. This covers the vast majority of colonial-era territories.\n\n2. **For pre-1886 entities** (Papal States, Italian pre-unification, early Ottoman provinces): Either pay for **Euratlas** (150 EUR/century) or **Centennia CRE** ($75/date, $3,125 for 1789-1939), or use **Aourednik Historical Basemaps** (free but coarse/unverified).\n\n3. **For sub-national colonial divisions**: Use **GADM** modern boundaries as proxies, **MPIDR Census Mosaic** for European empire internals (Austria-Hungary, Germany, Serbia), **CHGIS** for Chinese provinces, **HGIS de las Indias** for Spanish colonial America, and **Imperiia** for Russian Empire.\n\n4. **For gap-filling**: Use **David Rumsey** georeferenced scans + manual digitization in QGIS for any territories not available as vectors."}],"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1388,"cache_read_input_tokens":55533,"output_tokens":4479,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1388},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CZNbQ8K23pWP3MT6f8XsD","type":"assistant","uuid":"0faa84c7-9a27-4952-b28c-fbce4715f3f4","timestamp":"2026-03-24T21:13:54.394Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/home/catalin/WHEP-refactor-polities","sessionId":"b5ea2b37-3948-44f5-9e01-2ef45e84c64c","version":"2.1.81","gitBranch":"lbm364dl/refactor-polities","slug":"sequential-inventing-treehouse"}
